message.content: <local-command-caveat>Caveat: The messages below were generated by the user while running local commands. DO NOT respond to these messages or otherwise consider them in your response unless the user explicitly asks you to.</local-command-caveat>
<local-command-stdout>Set model to [1mFable 5[22m and saved as your default for new sessions</local-command-stdout>

Hey I want you to create a plan for how you would improve the session management and settings in keating. Currently settings are in several different categories and I want a component that is much easier to understand. Similarily, session components have a bunch of nested and duplicate serving interfaces that I would like to unify that is ideal for use in both mobile and desktop interfaces. The interfaces for mobile should utilize the realestate of the screen well as well. The settings should be simple. If you have suggestions for other things to improve along the way feel free to add these in an addendum as stretch goals.

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

## project
Project management CLI — init, list, doctor, status, lint, sync, register, history, templates, resume. Use project_init to scaffold new projects with AI-powered defaults. Use project_doctor for a full project intelligence report. Use project_list to see all projects across machines. Use project_sync with all=true to register all ~/Projects dirs in TrailBase. Use project_resume to continue interrupted sessions (e.g. after timeout).

Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud (requires claude.ai account access)). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).

<result>I have a thorough picture of session management. Here is my report.

## Session Management in the Keating Web App

### 1. Session UI components (with duplication)

There are **three parallel session-list interfaces** plus a shared-session viewer, all reading the same metadata store:

| File | Lines | Purpose |
|------|-------|---------|
| `web/src/components/SessionSidebar.tsx` | 434 | **Desktop** resizable left sidebar (drag-to-resize 288–640px, collapsed 56px strip). Renders a flattened, collapsible **fork tree** (`role="tree"`) with search, new-session, fork, load. Below `MD_BREAKPOINT = 1024px` it does **not** render its own list — it delegates entirely to `SessionCardGrid` (lines 215–229). |
| `web/src/components/SessionCardGrid.tsx` | 252 | **Mobile/tablet** full-screen "Apple-Journal-style" masonry card grid. Own search, own reload/`keating:sessions-changed` listener; renders `SessionCard` + `ForkMapCard`, builds fork tree via `buildSessionTree`. Supports rename/delete/fork inline. |
| `web/src/components/SessionManagerDialog.tsx` | 363 | The **"Manage" dialog** — a modal on small screens, a left **side panel** at ≥768px (`isSidePanel` via `matchMedia("(min-width: 768px)")`, lines 86–93). Full CRUD: load, fork, AI-suggest-title, rename (gated on `aiGeneratedTitle`), delete-with-confirm. Also renders the same flattened fork tree. |
| `web/src/pages/SharedSession.tsx` | (route) | Read-only viewer for a shared session loaded from URL hash; can fork a shared session into local storage. Lazy-loaded route in `web/src/App.tsx:22`. |

Supporting/leaf components:
- `web/src/components/SessionCard.tsx` (328) — individual masonry card (used only by the grid); fork badge, hero visuals.
- `web/src/components/ForkMapCard.tsx` (232) — renders a fork-tree mini-map card inside the grid.
- `web/src/components/ForkBanner.tsx` — banner shown in `Chat.tsx` when viewing a fork (link back to parent).
- `web/src/components/session-card-visuals.ts`, `web/src/components/fork-map-layout.ts` — masonry/layout helpers.

### 2. Storage & sync

- **Hook / wiring:** `web/src/hooks/keating-storage.ts` (87) constructs the stores (`SessionsStore` from `@earendil-works/pi-web-ui`), picks a backend, and exports the singleton `sessions` used everywhere. Also exports `updateSessionTitle` (re-derives metadata and dispatches `keating:sessions-changed`, lines 61–87).
- **Backend selection (lines 24–36):** if `hasP2PBackend() && window.keatingP2P` → `P2PStorageBackend`, else `IndexedDBStorageBackend` (dbName `"keating"`).
  - `web/src/lib/cloud-storage-backend.ts` (194) — IndexedDB implementation (misnamed "cloud"; it is the local IDB backend).
  - `web/src/lib/p2p-storage-backend.ts` (130) — proxies all ops over the Electron preload `window.keatingP2P` IPC bridge to a main-process Hyperbee/Hyperswarm P2PStore (desktop peer sync).
- **Metadata derivation:** `web/src/hooks/session-metadata.ts` (104) — `createSessionId`, `truncateAtForkPoint` (fork logic), `sessionTitle`, `sessionPreview`, `sessionUsage`, `sessionModelMetadata`.
- **Types:** `web/src/types/session.ts` (51) — `SessionMetadata` and `SessionData` (note `parentSessionId`/`forkedAt` for fork lineage, `aiGeneratedTitle` gate).
- **Keating domain storage:** `web/src/keating/storage.ts` (1178, `KeatingStorage`) — separate store for artifacts/goals/etc., initialized in `getInitPromise()`.
- **Sharing (cloud publish):** `web/src/keating/shared-sessions.ts` (272) — `saveSharedSession`, `sharedSessionUrl`, `loadSharedSessionFromUrl`, `forkSharedSession`, `listCachedSharedSessions`; codec in `web/src/keating/share-codec.ts`.

### 3. Where nesting/duplication exists

- **List rendered in three places** off one `sessions.getAllMetadata()` source: `SessionSidebar` (desktop tree), `SessionCardGrid` (mobile grid), `SessionManagerDialog` (manage dialog/side panel). Each independently: keeps its own `items` state, its own search box, and its own `keating:sessions-changed` reload listener.
- **`buildSessionTree`/`flattenSessionTree`** (`web/src/components/session-tree.ts`, 75) is invoked separately by all three.
- **Nested delegation:** `SessionSidebar` embeds `SessionCardGrid` for non-desktop widths (line 218), so the sidebar and grid are coupled — the "sidebar" is really two components behind one breakpoint switch, while the dialog is a third fully independent implementation.
- **Two side-panel patterns coexist:** `SessionManagerDialog` becomes a left side panel at ≥768px, and `SessionSidebar` is a separate left panel at ≥1024px — overlapping responsibilities in the 768–1024px range.

### 4. Mobile vs desktop handling

- Breakpoints: `SessionSidebar` uses `MD_BREAKPOINT = 1024` via `window.matchMedia` (lines 24, 106–120); `SessionManagerDialog` uses `768px` for modal-vs-side-panel (lines 86–93). `Chat.tsx` uses `isWideViewport` for the artifact panel.
- Mobile drawer state lives in the store `web/src/stores/keating-agent-store.ts`: `mobileSidebarOpen`, `toggleMobileSidebar`, `closeMobileSidebar` (lines 83, 110, 131–132), plus persisted desktop `sessionSidebarCollapsed` (localStorage key `keating:session-sidebar-collapsed`, lines 11, 82, 122–129).
- Pattern: no dedicated sheet library — mobile "drawer" is `SessionCardGrid` gated by `mobileOpen` (returns `null` when closed, `SessionSidebar.tsx:215-216`); dialog side-panel uses fixed-position `pointer-events` shell classes (lines 183–188).

### 5. Chat.tsx integration

`web/src/pages/Chat.tsx` (574) does **not** own session logic — it consumes the `useKeatingAgent()` hook (`web/src/hooks/useKeatingAgent.tsx`):
- Destructures `newSession, shareSession, sessionSidebar, forkingSessionId, forkInfo, openOriginalSession, mobileSidebarOpen, toggleMobileSidebar` (Chat.tsx:47–63).
- Renders the sidebar node inline in the flex layout: `{sessionSidebar}` at `Chat.tsx:482`, next to `AssistantChatPanel`.
- Mobile toggle button wired to `toggleMobileSidebar` (Chat.tsx:202–207); "New session" buttons call `newSession` (226–229, 329–333); share button calls `shareSession` and fires `posthog.capture('session_shared')` (154–168); `ForkBanner` rendered from `forkInfo` (445–448).
- The dialog is rendered separately by the hook itself (`{dialogs}` at Chat.tsx:555, produced in `useKeatingAgent.tsx:950`).

**The hook is the real session controller** — `web/src/hooks/useKeatingAgent.tsx`:
- Builds both `sessionManagerDialogElement` (lines 773–792) and `sessionSidebarElement` (lines 794–812), passing shared callbacks `loadSession`, `forkSession`, `newSession`.
- Core session ops: `newSession` (581), `shareSession` (605), `loadSession` (620), `forkSession` (666), title suggestion (720), latest-session restore on mount with timeout (915–920).

### Structural takeaway
One data layer (`sessions` store + `SessionMetadata`/fork tree) fans out into three independently-stateful list UIs (desktop tree sidebar, mobile card grid, manage dialog/side panel), orchestrated centrally by `useKeatingAgent`. The clearest consolidation targets are the duplicated `items`/search/`keating:sessions-changed` reload logic and the overlapping left-panel patterns (`SessionSidebar` vs `SessionManagerDialog` side panel) across the 768/1024px breakpoints.</result>

## Settings UI structure — keating web app

### 1. Settings components/files (all under `web/src`)

| File | Lines | Purpose |

<result>I have a thorough picture of session management. Here is my report.

## Session Management in the Keating Web App

### 1. Session UI components (with duplication)

There are **three parallel session-list interfaces** plus a shared-session viewer, all reading the same metadata store:

| File | Lines | Purpose |
|------|-------|---------|
| `web/src/components/SessionSidebar.tsx` | 434 | **Desktop** resizable left sidebar (drag-to-resize 288–640px, collapsed 56px strip). Renders a flattened, collapsible **fork tree** (`role="tree"`) with search, new-session, fork, load. Below `MD_BREAKPOINT = 1024px` it does **not** render its own list — it delegates entirely to `SessionCardGrid` (lines 215–229). |
| `web/src/components/SessionCardGrid.tsx` | 252 | **Mobile/tablet** full-screen "Apple-Journal-style" masonry card grid. Own search, own reload/`keating:sessions-changed` listener; renders `SessionCard` + `ForkMapCard`, builds fork tree via `buildSessionTree`. Supports rename/delete/fork inline. |
| `web/src/components/SessionManagerDialog.tsx` | 363 | The **"Manage" dialog** — a modal on small screens, a left **side panel** at ≥768px (`isSidePanel` via `matchMedia("(min-width: 768px)")`, lines 86–93). Full CRUD: load, fork, AI-suggest-title, rename (gated on `aiGeneratedTitle`), delete-with-confirm. Also renders the same flattened fork tree. |
| `web/src/pages/SharedSession.tsx` | (route) | Read-only viewer for a shared session loaded from URL hash; can fork a shared session into local storage. Lazy-loaded route in `web/src/App.tsx:22`. |

Supporting/leaf components:
- `web/src/components/SessionCard.tsx` (328) — individual masonry card (used only by the grid); fork badge, hero visuals.
- `web/src/components/ForkMapCard.tsx` (232) — renders a fork-tree mini-map card inside the grid.
- `web/src/components/ForkBanner.tsx` — banner shown in `Chat.tsx` when viewing a fork (link back to parent).
- `web/src/components/session-card-visuals.ts`, `web/src/components/fork-map-layout.ts` — masonry/layout helpers.

### 2. Storage & sync

- **Hook / wiring:** `web/src/hooks/keating-storage.ts` (87) constructs the stores (`SessionsStore` from `@earendil-works/pi-web-ui`), picks a backend, and exports the singleton `sessions` used everywhere. Also exports `updateSessionTitle` (re-derives metadata and dispatches `keating:sessions-changed`, lines 61–87).
- **Backend selection (lines 24–36):** if `hasP2PBackend() && window.keatingP2P` → `P2PStorageBackend`, else `IndexedDBStorageBackend` (dbName `"keating"`).
  - `web/src/lib/cloud-storage-backend.ts` (194) — IndexedDB implementation (misnamed "cloud"; it is the local IDB backend).
  - `web/src/lib/p2p-storage-backend.ts` (130) — proxies all ops over the Electron preload `window.keatingP2P` IPC bridge to a main-process Hyperbee/Hyperswarm P2PStore (desktop peer sync).
- **Metadata derivation:** `web/src/hooks/session-metadata.ts` (104) — `createSessionId`, `truncateAtForkPoint` (fork logic), `sessionTitle`, `sessionPreview`, `sessionUsage`, `sessionModelMetadata`.
- **Types:** `web/src/types/session.ts` (51) — `SessionMetadata` and `SessionData` (note `parentSessionId`/`forkedAt` for fork lineage, `aiGeneratedTitle` gate).
- **Keating domain storage:** `web/src/keating/storage.ts` (1178, `KeatingStorage`) — separate store for artifacts/goals/etc., initialized in `getInitPromise()`.
- **Sharing (cloud publish):** `web/src/keating/shared-sessions.ts` (272) — `saveSharedSession`, `sharedSessionUrl`, `loadSharedSessionFromUrl`, `forkSharedSession`, `listCachedSharedSessions`; codec in `web/src/keating/share-codec.ts`.

### 3. Where nesting/duplication exists

- **List rendered in three places** off one `sessions.getAllMetadata()` source: `SessionSidebar` (desktop tree), `SessionCardGrid` (mobile grid), `SessionManagerDialog` (manage dialog/side panel). Each independently: keeps its own `items` state, its own search box, and its own `keating:sessions-changed` reload listener.
- **`buildSessionTree`/`flattenSessionTree`** (`web/src/components/session-tree.ts`, 75) is invoked separately by all three.
- **Nested delegation:** `SessionSidebar` embeds `SessionCardGrid` for non-desktop widths (line 218), so the sidebar and grid are coupled — the "sidebar" is really two components behind one breakpoint switch, while the dialog is a third fully independent implementation.
- **Two side-panel patterns coexist:** `SessionManagerDialog` becomes a left side panel at ≥768px, and `SessionSidebar` is a separate left panel at ≥1024px — overlapping responsibilities in the 768–1024px range.

### 4. Mobile vs desktop handling

- Breakpoints: `SessionSidebar` uses `MD_BREAKPOINT = 1024` via `window.matchMedia` (lines 24, 106–120); `SessionManagerDialog` uses `768px` for modal-vs-side-panel (lines 86–93). `Chat.tsx` uses `isWideViewport` for the artifact panel.
- Mobile drawer state lives in the store `web/src/stores/keating-agent-store.ts`: `mobileSidebarOpen`, `toggleMobileSidebar`, `closeMobileSidebar` (lines 83, 110, 131–132), plus persisted desktop `sessionSidebarCollapsed` (localStorage key `keating:session-sidebar-collapsed`, lines 11, 82, 122–129).
- Pattern: no dedicated sheet library — mobile "drawer" is `SessionCardGrid` gated by `mobileOpen` (returns `null` when closed, `SessionSidebar.tsx:215-216`); dialog side-panel uses fixed-position `pointer-events` shell classes (lines 183–188).

### 5. Chat.tsx integration

`web/src/pages/Chat.tsx` (574) does **not** own session logic — it consumes the `useKeatingAgent()` hook (`web/src/hooks/useKeatingAgent.tsx`):
- Destructures `newSession, shareSession, sessionSidebar, forkingSessionId, forkInfo, openOriginalSession, mobileSidebarOpen, toggleMobileSidebar` (Chat.tsx:47–63).
- Renders the sidebar node inline in the flex layout: `{sessionSidebar}` at `Chat.tsx:482`, next to `AssistantChatPanel`.
- Mobile toggle button wired to `toggleMobileSidebar` (Chat.tsx:202–207); "New session" buttons call `newSession` (226–229, 329–333); share button calls `shareSession` and fires `posthog.capture('session_shared')` (154–168); `ForkBanner` rendered from `forkInfo` (445–448).
- The dialog is rendered separately by the hook itself (`{dialogs}` at Chat.tsx:555, produced in `useKeatingAgent.tsx:950`).

**The hook is the real session controller** — `web/src/hooks/useKeatingAgent.tsx`:
- Builds both `sessionManagerDialogElement` (lines 773–792) and `sessionSidebarElement` (lines 794–812), passing shared callbacks `loadSession`, `forkSession`, `newSession`.
- Core session ops: `newSession` (581), `shareSession` (605), `loadSession` (620), `forkSession` (666), title suggestion (720), latest-session restore on mount with timeout (915–920).

### Structural takeaway
One data layer (`sessions` store + `SessionMetadata`/fork tree) fans out into three independently-stateful list UIs (desktop tree sidebar, mobile card grid, manage dialog/side panel), orchestrated centrally by `useKeatingAgent`. The clearest consolidation targets are the duplicated `items`/search/`keating:sessions-changed` reload logic and the overlapping left-panel patterns (`SessionSidebar` vs `SessionManagerDialog` side panel) across the 768/1024px breakpoints.</result>

- Tabs assembled in `web/src/hooks/useKeatingAgent.tsx:816-828`: ProvidersModelsTab (973 lines: Cloud Providers/keys, Web Search, Provider Visibility, My Models, Custom Providers), TeacherPersonaTab (102), SpeechSettingsTab (363), KeatingUiSettingsTab (398: Chat Interface, Share Links, Animation Renderer, Reasoning Level, Image generation), ProxyTab (60).
- Persistence fragmented: localStorage keys `keating_ui_settings` (ui-settings.ts, event `keating:ui-settings-changed`), `keating:teacher-persona` (persona.ts), `keating:web:speech` (speech.ts); IndexedDB pi-web-ui SettingsStore for `proxy.*`; providerKeys/customProviders stores. `hooks/keating-storage.ts` wires backends (IndexedDB or P2P).
- Known issues: Interface-owned `keating_ui_settings` blob backs Providers-tab UI (customModels, hiddenProviders, recentModels); two overlapping toggles `googleGrounding` vs `webSearch`; ProvidersModelsTab manually reloads via `setSettings(loadKeatingUiSettings())` (:161,191,471) instead of `subscribeKeatingUiSettings`; SettingsSectionNav used in Providers+Speech but not Interface; shared `SettingRow.tsx` and `Toggle.tsx` exist.

## Goal 2: Unified adaptive SessionBrowser

Current state:
- Three parallel UIs off one `sessions.getAllMetadata()` source: `web/src/components/SessionSidebar.tsx` (434, desktop resizable tree sidebar, ≥1024px; below that delegates to SessionCardGrid at lines 215-229), `SessionCardGrid.tsx` (252, mobile masonry card grid with SessionCard + ForkMapCard), `SessionManagerDialog.tsx` (363, modal <768px / left side panel ≥768px, full CRUD: load, fork, AI title suggest, rename gated on aiGeneratedTitle, delete-with-confirm).
- Each keeps its own items state, search box, and `keating:sessions-changed` reload listener; each calls `buildSessionTree`/`flattenSessionTree` from `session-tree.ts` (75).
- Orchestrated by `web/src/hooks/useKeatingAgent.tsx`: builds sessionManagerDialogElement (:773-792) and sessionSidebarElement (:794-812); ops newSession (:581), shareSession (:605), loadSession (:620), forkSession (:666), title suggestion (:720). `Chat.tsx` renders `{sessionSidebar}` at :482 and `{dialogs}` at :555.
- Mobile drawer state in `web/src/stores/keating-agent-store.ts`: mobileSidebarOpen etc; desktop collapse persisted at `keating:session-sidebar-collapsed`.
- Breakpoint mismatch: dialog side-panel at ≥768, sidebar at ≥1024.

User-approved direction: **one adaptive `SessionBrowser` component** — desktop: left sidebar (keep resizable + tree + collapse); mobile: full-screen sheet with the card-grid visuals; delete `SessionManagerDialog` entirely and fold its CRUD (rename, delete-confirm, AI-title) into SessionBrowser; single shared data hook (e.g. `useSessions()` encapsulating items/search/tree/reload-on-`keating:sessions-changed`). Unify on one breakpoint (1024px? justify). Preserve fork tree, ForkMapCard, SharedSession flow, P2P backend compatibility.

## Constraints
- Incremental, reviewable steps; keep git history sane.
- Reuse: SettingRow, Toggle, SettingsSectionNav, session-tree.ts, session-card-visuals.ts, existing keating-agent-store.
- Mobile must use screen real estate well (full-screen sheet, safe-area, tap targets already handled in app.css).
- Tests exist at web/src/test/ (e.g. ui-settings.test.ts); note what tests to add/update.
- There are uncommitted changes in the working tree (p2p/electron work in progress) — plan must not assume clean tree but shouldn't touch desktop/ or packages/p2p-core.

## Deliverable
A step-by-step implementation plan: ordered phases, files created/modified/deleted per phase, key component APIs (props/hooks signatures), migration notes for any storage changes, risks, and a verification checklist. Also include a short "stretch goals" addendum list (things you notice worth improving but out of scope — e.g. the googleGrounding/webSearch merge, ProvidersModelsTab decomposition, persistence unification if deferred).

<result># Implementation Plan: Settings Consolidation + Unified SessionBrowser

I validated the codebase state described in the brief by reading the key files. Notable findings that shape the plan:

- `SessionCard.tsx` **already implements** inline rename (Pencil), delete-with-confirm (Trash + `confirmingDelete`), via `onRename`/`onDelete` props — the mobile grid already has most of the CRUD that `SessionManagerDialog` provides. The only capability the dialog exclusively owns is **AI title suggestion** (`onSuggestTitle`) and the desktop-visible rename/delete (the desktop `SessionSidebar` rows have neither).
- `openSessions`/`sessionsDialog` have no consumers outside `useKeatingAgent.tsx` (grep confirmed), so removing the dialog is contained to that hook plus the two "Manage" buttons in `SessionSidebar.tsx:289` and `SessionCardGrid.tsx:145`.
- `SettingsDialog.tsx` already renders the mobile `<select>` unconditionally in the header row (visible when the `hidden sm:flex` sidebar is hidden) — swapping to chips is a small, isolated change.
- `SessionCardGrid` fetches artifact heroes (`buildArtifactHeroMap`) in the same reload as metadata; the shared hook must support that.
- Uncommitted changes touch `Chat.tsx`, `useKeatingAgent.tsx` adjacents (`keating-storage.ts`, `App.tsx`, `app.css`) — plan works on top of the current tree; commits should be scoped per phase so p2p WIP stays separable.

---

## Part 1 — Settings: 5 tabs → 3 grouped tabs

### Grouping decision (and justification)

| Tab | Contents | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| **Models & Providers** | Cloud provider keys, Web Search, Provider Visibility, My Models, Custom Providers, **Proxy** | Everything about *how the app reaches LLMs*. Proxy belongs here, not "App": it exists to route provider requests (CORS/relay); a user debugging a failing provider key will look for proxy config next to keys. Keeps the 973-line ProvidersModelsTab untouched. |
| **Learning** | Teacher Persona, Speech & Voice | Both configure the *tutoring experience* (who the teacher is, how it speaks). Natural pair; both are small components already. |
| **App** | Chat Interface toggles, Share Links, Animation Renderer, Reasoning Level, Image Generation, Font | Exactly the current KeatingUiSettingsTab. Reasoning level stays here rather than Models & Providers because it is a chat-behavior default persisted in `keating_ui_settings`, and moving it would either split the Interface tab or drag `ui-settings` state into ProvidersModelsTab — churn with no user benefit. |

This grouping requires **zero decomposition** of existing tab components: the two merged tabs are composed by concatenation, with section navs merged.

### Phase 1.1 — SettingsDialog mobile chips (isolated, ship first)

**Modified:** `web/src/components/SettingsDialog.tsx`

- Replace the header `<select>` (lines 76-85) with a horizontal chip row: `sm:hidden flex gap-1.5 overflow-x-auto` of pill buttons (same active styling as the sidebar buttons, `whitespace-nowrap`, `min-h-9` for tap targets). With 3 tabs, chips fit a 320px viewport; `overflow-x-auto` covers safety. Keep the desktop sidebar unchanged.
- Add `defaultTabId?: string` prop (optional, future-proofing deep links like "open proxy settings"); keep index-based state internally, resolve id → index once.
- Chips are plain buttons < 100% width — they're inside `[role=dialog]`, so per `app.css:402-416` they'd stretch on mobile; give them the existing `dialog-compact-button` class to opt out (reuse, no CSS change).

### Phase 1.2 — Section-nav prefix collision guard + composed tabs

**Created:**
- `web/src/components/settings/LearningTab.tsx` (~40 lines):
  ```tsx
  export function LearningTab({ onSpeechSettingsChange }: { onSpeechSettingsChange: (s: SpeechSettings) => void }) {
    // <SettingsSectionNav sections={[{id:"persona",...},{id:"speech-enable",...}, ...speech sections]}/>
    // <section id="settings-section-persona"><h3>Teacher Persona</h3><TeacherPersonaTab/></section>
    // <SpeechSettingsTab onSettingsChange={...} hideNav />
  }
  ```
  Speech already has its own `SettingsSectionNav` with `settings-section-speech-*` ids (`SpeechSettingsTab.tsx:136`). Add an optional `hideNav?: boolean` prop to `SpeechSettingsTab` so LearningTab renders one merged nav (persona + the 5 speech section entries) instead of two stacked navs. TeacherPersonaTab (102 lines) gets wrapped in a section div with a heading — no internal changes.
- `web/src/components/settings/ModelsProvidersTab.tsx` (~30 lines): renders `<ProvidersModelsTab extraSections={[{id:"proxy", label:"Proxy"}]} />` … simpler: renders `<ProvidersModelsTab />` followed by a `#settings-section-proxy` section wrapping `<ProxyTab />`. Add an optional `extraNavSections?: SectionDef[]` prop to `ProvidersModelsTab` so its existing nav (`ProvidersModelsTab` nav block around line 267) includes the Proxy entry. ProxyTab (60 lines) is unchanged.

**Modified:**
- `web/src/hooks/useKeatingAgent.tsx:816-828` — tabs array becomes:
  ```tsx
  tabs={[
    { id: "models", label: "Models & Providers", component: <ModelsProvidersTab /> },
    { id: "learning", label: "Learning", component: <LearningTab onSpeechSettingsChange={setSpeechSettings} /> },
    { id: "app", label: "App", component: <KeatingUiSettingsTab /> },
  ]}
  ```
- `web/src/components/SpeechSettingsTab.tsx` — `hideNav` prop + export its `SECTIONS` const.
- `web/src/components/ProvidersModelsTab.tsx` — `extraNavSections` prop (nav array spread only; ~3-line diff).
- `web/src/components/KeatingUiSettingsTab.tsx` — add a `SettingsSectionNav` (Interface / Share Links / Animation / Reasoning / Images) for parity, wrapping its existing groups in `id="settings-section-*"` divs with `scroll-mt-20`. Purely additive.

Old tab files are **not deleted** — `TeacherPersonaTab`, `SpeechSettingsTab`, `ProxyTab`, `ProvidersModelsTab` remain as the section building blocks; only their top-level registration moves.

### Phase 1.3 — Persistence: minimal fix now, unification deferred

**Recommendation: do not unify persistence in this refactor.** The three localStorage keys + IndexedDB SettingsStore each have working subscribe/migration paths and P2P backend wiring in `keating-storage.ts`; merging them risks data migration bugs for zero UI payoff. Do only the known-bug fix:

**Modified:** `web/src/components/ProvidersModelsTab.tsx` — replace the three manual `setSettings(loadKeatingUiSettings())` reload sites (:161, :191, :471) with a single `useEffect` that calls `subscribeKeatingUiSettings(setSettings)`. This fixes stale state when the App tab (or another window) mutates `keating_ui_settings` while the Providers tab is mounted — newly relevant because grouped tabs make cross-tab edits within one dialog session more likely.

Optionally introduce `web/src/hooks/use-ui-settings.ts`:
```ts
export function useKeatingUiSettings(): [KeatingUiSettings, (patch: Partial<KeatingUiSettings>) => void]
```
wrapping `loadKeatingUiSettings` + `subscribeKeatingUiSettings` + `saveKeatingUiSettings`, adopted by `ProvidersModelsTab` and `KeatingUiSettingsTab` only. Low-risk, ~30 lines, kills the duplicated load/subscribe boilerplate. Everything else (persona.ts, speech.ts, proxy SettingsStore) stays as-is.

**Tests:** extend `web/src/test/ui-settings.test.ts` with a subscribe-propagation test (save → subscriber invoked with merged settings); add `use-ui-settings.test.ts` if the hook is added (vitest + happy-dom, render via existing test patterns).

---

## Part 2 — Unified adaptive SessionBrowser

### Breakpoint decision: **1024px**, single source of truth

Justification: the desktop sidebar needs 288–640px (`SIDEBAR_MIN_W/MAX_W`) alongside a usable chat column; at 768–1023px that squeezes chat below ~450px. `SessionSidebar` already uses 1024 (`MD_BREAKPOINT`); the 768px side-panel mode only exists in the dialog being deleted, so standardizing on 1024 is also the minimal-churn choice. Export it once: `export const SESSION_BROWSER_BREAKPOINT = 1024` from the new component (or `session-tree.ts` sibling module) and use a single `useMediaQuery`-style hook.

### Phase 2.1 — `useSessions()` shared data hook

**Created:** `web/src/hooks/use-sessions.ts`

```ts
export interface UseSessionsResult {
  items: SessionMetadata[];
  loading: boolean;
  query: string;
  setQuery: (q: string) => void;
  /** non-null while searching: flat, recency-sorted, capped */
  flatResults: SessionMetadata[] | null;
  /** tree roots when not searching */
  roots: SessionTreeNode[];
  heroes: Map<string, ArtifactHero>;        // empty Map unless withHeroes
  reload: () => Promise<void>;
  rename: (id: string, title: string, fromAi?: boolean) => Promise<void>; // updateSessionTitle + dispatch keating:sessions-changed
  remove: (id: string) => Promise<void>;    // sessions.deleteSession + dispatch
  error: string | null;
}
export function useSessions(opts?: { withHeroes?: boolean; flatLimit?: number }): UseSessionsResult
```

- Encapsulates: `sessions.getAllMetadata()`, `buildArtifactHeroMap(keatingStorage)` (only when `withHeroes`, catch → empty Map, matching `SessionCardGrid.tsx:76-79`), the `keating:sessions-changed` listener, the search filter (union of the three components' current predicates: title/preview/relative-date/messageCount), and `buildSessionTree`. Flattening stays in the consumer because collapsed-node state is desktop-only.
- `rename`/`remove` dispatch `keating:sessions-changed` themselves — this replaces the dialog's `onDeleted`/`onRenamed` callback plumbing in `useKeatingAgent.tsx:779-784` (which existed only to fire that same event).
- P2P compatibility: the hook only talks to the `sessions` facade from `keating-storage.ts`, same as today — backend-agnostic by construction.

**Tests:** `web/src/test/use-sessions.test.ts` — filter predicate + tree/flat switching + event-driven reload (mock the `sessions` module; the repo already mocks storage in `session-fork.test.ts`).

Commit Phase 2.1 alone (pure addition, no behavior change).

### Phase 2.2 — `SessionBrowser.tsx` (adaptive shell) + desktop CRUD parity

**Created:** `web/src/components/SessionBrowser.tsx`

```ts
export interface SessionBrowserProps {
  activeSessionId?: string;
  forkingSessionId?: string | null;
  forkedSessionId?: string | null;
  collapsed?: boolean;                       // desktop only
  onCollapsedChange?: (next: boolean) => void;
  mobileOpen?: boolean;                      // <1024 full-screen sheet visibility
  onMobileClose?: () => void;
  onLoad: (sessionId: string) => void | Promise<void>;
  onFork: (sessionId: string) => void | Promise<void>;
  onNewSession?: () => void;
  onSuggestTitle?: (sessionId: string) => Promise<string>;  // AI title, threaded to both variants
}
```

Structure:
- Top-level component owns `useSessions({ withHeroes: !isDesktop })` and the breakpoint media query, then renders `<DesktopSessionPanel>` or `<MobileSessionSheet>` (both in the same file or as `SessionBrowser/` siblings — prefer two files `SessionBrowserDesktop.tsx` / `SessionBrowserSheet.tsx` given SessionSidebar is already 434 lines).
- **Desktop panel**: lift the current `SessionSidebar` desktop code verbatim (collapsed strip, resizable width incl. `keating_session_sidebar_width`, tree with `keating:session-tree-collapsed` persistence, fork buttons) minus its data fetching (now from the hook) and minus the "Manage" button. Add per-row CRUD to reach parity with the deleted dialog: a hover-revealed action cluster (Fork — existing; Sparkles → `onSuggestTitle` flowing into inline rename input prefilled; Pencil → inline rename, enabled per the existing `aiGeneratedTitle` gate; Trash → inline confirm strip). Port the rename/delete/suggest state machine from `SessionManagerDialog.tsx:101-181` (busy/error/pendingDelete/aiSuggestion states) — this logic moves, it is not rewritten. To keep rows uncluttered, collapse the four actions behind a `MoreHorizontal` popover on rows, with Fork remaining a first-class button (current behavior).
- **Mobile sheet**: lift `SessionCardGrid` wholesale (it is already a full-screen `fixed inset-0` sheet with backdrop, masonry, `SessionCard` + `ForkMapCard`, and rename/delete). Changes: remove the "Manage" button; add a Sparkles action to `SessionCard`'s action row wired to `onSuggestTitle` (prefill its existing rename input); route rename/delete through the hook's `rename`/`remove` instead of its local `updateSessionTitle`/`deleteSession` calls; keep the `role="dialog"` avoidance comment/behavior (app.css full-width-button rule). Add `pb-[env(safe-area-inset-bottom)]` on the scroller if not already covered by app.css.

**Modified:** `web/src/components/SessionCard.tsx` — optional `onSuggestTitle?: (id: string) => Promise<string>` prop; when present, show Sparkles button that awaits it and enters rename mode with the draft. `ForkMapCard` threading is optional (its rows can omit suggest; note as stretch if its API makes it noisy).

Commit Phase 2.2 with `SessionSidebar`/`SessionCardGrid` still present (SessionBrowser not yet wired) — reviewable as pure addition, or alternatively fold 2.2+2.3 into one commit if dead-code-in-tree between commits is unwanted. Recommended: keep them separate but wire in the same PR.

### Phase 2.3 — Wire in, delete the old trio

**Modified:**
- `web/src/hooks/useKeatingAgent.tsx`:
  - Replace `sessionSidebarElement` (:794-812) with `<SessionBrowser ... onSuggestTitle={suggestSessionTitle} />` (same props minus `onOpenSessions`, plus `onSuggestTitle`).
  - Delete `sessionManagerDialogElement` (:773-792), the `SessionManagerDialog` import (:11), and `sessionsDialog = useDialogState()` (:182).
  - Repoint `openSessions` (:769-771, exported at :119/:971) instead of deleting it — keep the public API stable: on <1024 it calls the store's `toggleMobileSidebar`/open action; on desktop it calls `setSidebarCollapsed(false)`. (No external consumers found, but the hook's return type is public surface; cheap to preserve.)
- `web/src/pages/Chat.tsx` — remove the dialog element from the `{dialogs}` fragment if listed individually; `{sessionSidebar}` render point (:482) unchanged (rename the variable to `sessionBrowser` for clarity).
- `web/src/stores/keating-agent-store.ts` — no changes needed: `mobileSidebarOpen` and `keating:session-sidebar-collapsed` persistence are reused as-is.

**Deleted:**
- `web/src/components/SessionManagerDialog.tsx`
- `web/src/components/SessionSidebar.tsx` (logic relocated into SessionBrowser desktop variant)
- `web/src/components/SessionCardGrid.tsx` (relocated into sheet variant)
- Remove now-dead `session-manager-dialog` / `session-manager-side-panel` CSS classes from `web/src/app.css` (careful: app.css has uncommitted p2p edits — make surgical removals, don't reformat).

**Migration notes (storage):** none required. All persisted keys survive unchanged: `keating_session_sidebar_width`, `keating:session-tree-collapsed`, `keating:session-sidebar-collapsed`. No session-data schema changes.

**Tests:** update/keep `session-fork.test.ts`, `fork-map-layout.test.ts`, `session-card-visuals.test.ts` (should pass untouched — logic modules unchanged). Add `use-sessions.test.ts` (Phase 2.1). If any test imports `SessionCardGrid`/`SessionSidebar` (grep before deleting), update imports to SessionBrowser internals.

---

## Ordering & commits

1. **Commit 1:** Phase 1.1 — SettingsDialog chips.
2. **Commit 2:** Phase 1.2 — grouped tabs (LearningTab, ModelsProvidersTab, nav merges, tab registration).
3. **Commit 3:** Phase 1.3 — subscribe fix (+ optional `useKeatingUiSettings`) + test.
4. **Commit 4:** Phase 2.1 — `useSessions` hook + test.
5. **Commit 5:** Phases 2.2 + 2.3 — SessionBrowser, wiring, deletions (single commit so the tree never ships two parallel session UIs; the diff is large but mostly moved code — note "moved from SessionSidebar/SessionCardGrid" in the message).

Each commit leaves `bun run build` / `bunx vitest` green. Do not touch `desktop/` or `packages/p2p-core`; stage only the files listed (working tree has unrelated WIP in `Chat.tsx`/`app.css`/`useKeatingAgent.tsx` — coordinate or use `git add -p` equivalent staging per hunk).

## Risks

- **Uncommitted WIP overlap** in `useKeatingAgent.tsx`, `Chat.tsx`, `app.css`, `keating-storage.ts` — highest practical risk; rebase-style conflicts within one tree. Mitigation: land settings phases (low overlap) first; confirm p2p WIP status before Phase 2.3.
- **Desktop CRUD ergonomics**: adding rename/delete/suggest to dense tree rows can clutter; popover cluster mitigates. Verify tap targets ≥ 40px per app.css conventions.
- **aiGeneratedTitle rename gate**: the dialog disabled Pencil until an AI title existed (`SessionManagerDialog.tsx:294`), but `SessionCard` allows free rename. Unify on the *card* behavior (free rename, `updateSessionTitle(id, title, fromAi || session.aiGeneratedTitle)`) — the gate appears to be a product quirk, flag in PR for confirmation.
- **`role="dialog"` button-stretch rule** (`app.css:402-416`): the sheet must keep the non-dialog-role workaround; the desktop panel is an `<aside>` (unaffected); SettingsDialog chips need `dialog-compact-button`.
- **Hero map cost on desktop**: `withHeroes` gated to mobile keeps the desktop sidebar reload cheap (current behavior).
- **P2P/cloud backends**: all access stays behind `sessions`/`keatingStorage` facades; no direct IndexedDB in new code.

## Verification checklist

- Settings: 3 tabs on desktop sidebar and as mobile chips (<640px); chip row scrolls if cramped; section nav jump-links work in all three tabs; provider key add/remove, custom model add, proxy save, persona save, speech test, and every App toggle still persist across reload; editing App-tab settings while Models tab mounted reflects immediately (subscribe fix).
- Sessions desktop (≥1024): resize persists, collapse strip persists, tree expand/collapse persists, search flattens, load/fork/new work, rename/delete/AI-suggest work inline, fork animation classes (`session-fork-arrive`, "Forked" chip) intact.
- Sessions mobile (<1024): hamburger opens full-screen sheet; masonry with SessionCard heroes + ForkMapCard for forked roots; rename/delete/fork/suggest on cards; close on load; safe-area respected; no stretched buttons.
- Cross-cutting: fork from active session captures latest messages (snapshot-before-fork path unchanged); SharedSession share-link flow unchanged (`shareLinkMode` still read from ui-settings); works with P2P backend enabled; `bunx vitest` green; no console errors at 375px, 768px, 1024px, 1440px widths.

## Stretch goals (out of scope, noticed while exploring)

- Merge `googleGrounding` + `webSearch` into one setting with a one-time migration in `normalizeUiSettings`.
- Decompose the 973-line `ProvidersModelsTab.tsx` into per-section files under `components/settings/`.
- Full persistence unification: a `useKeatingSetting(key)` facade over localStorage keys + SettingsStore, enabling P2P sync of persona/speech/ui settings (currently only proxy/keys go through SettingsStore).
- Move `customModels`/`hiddenProviders`/`recentModels` out of the Interface-owned `keating_ui_settings` blob into a provider-scoped store.
- `ForkMapCard` AI-suggest support; keyboard navigation for the session tree (`role="tree"` exists but no arrow-key handling).
- Deep-link support `?settings=proxy` using the new `defaultTabId` prop.
- Replace ad-hoc `window.matchMedia` copies with one shared `useMediaQuery` hook (three implementations exist today).

### Critical Files for Implementation
- /home/diogenes/Projects/keating/web/src/hooks/useKeatingAgent.tsx
- /home/diogenes/Projects/keating/web/src/components/SessionSidebar.tsx
- /home/diogenes/Projects/keating/web/src/components/SessionCardGrid.tsx
- /home/diogenes/Projects/keating/web/src/components/SettingsDialog.tsx
- /home/diogenes/Projects/keating/web/src/components/SessionManagerDialog.tsx</result>

Notable findings that shape the plan: - `SessionCard.tsx` **already implements** inline rename (Pencil), delete-with-confirm (Trash + `confirmingDelete`), via `onRename`/`onDelete` props — the mobile grid already has most of the CRUD that `SessionManagerDialog` provides. With 3 tabs, chips fit a 320px viewport; `overflow-x-auto` covers safety. Teacher Persona

// } ``` Speech already has its own `SettingsSectionNav` with `settings-section-speech-*` ids (`SpeechSettingsTab.tsx:136`). Add an optional `hideNav?: boolean` prop to `SpeechSettingsTab` so LearningTab renders one merged nav (persona + the 5 speech section entries) instead of two stacked navs. TeacherPersonaTab (102 lines) gets wrapped in a section div with a heading — no internal changes. - `web/src/components/settings/ModelsProvidersTab.tsx` (~30 lines): renders `` … simpler: renders `` followed by a `#settings-section-proxy` section wrapping ``. ProxyTab (60 lines) is unchanged. **Modified:** - `web/src/hooks/useKeatingAgent.tsx:816-828` — tabs array becomes: ```tsx tabs={[ { id: "models", label: "Models & Providers", component: }, { id: "learning", label: "Learning", component: }, { id: "app", label: "App", component: }, ]} ``` - `web/src/components/SpeechSettingsTab.tsx` — `hideNav` prop + export its `SECTIONS` const. - `web/src/components/ProvidersModelsTab.tsx` — `extraNavSections` prop (nav array spread only; ~3-line diff). - `web/src/components/KeatingUiSettingsTab.tsx` — add a `SettingsSectionNav` (Interface / Share Links / Animation / Reasoning / Images) for parity, wrapping its existing groups in `id="settings-section-*"` divs with `scroll-mt-20`. Purely additive. Old tab files are **not deleted** — `TeacherPersonaTab`, `SpeechSettingsTab`, `ProxyTab`, `ProvidersModelsTab` remain as the section building blocks; only their top-level registration moves. ### Phase 1.3 — Persistence: minimal fix now, unification deferred **Recommendation: do not unify persistence in this refactor.** The three localStorage keys + IndexedDB SettingsStore each have working subscribe/migration paths and P2P backend wiring in `keating-storage.ts`; merging them risks data migration bugs for zero UI payoff. Do only the known-bug fix: **Modified:** `web/src/components/ProvidersModelsTab.tsx` — replace the three manual `setSettings(loadKeatingUiSettings())` reload sites (:161, :191, :471) with a single `useEffect` that calls `subscribeKeatingUiSettings(setSettings)`. This fixes stale state when the App tab (or another window) mutates `keating_ui_settings` while the Providers tab is mounted — newly relevant because grouped tabs make cross-tab edits within one dialog session more likely. Optionally introduce `web/src/hooks/use-ui-settings.ts`: ```ts export function useKeatingUiSettings(): [KeatingUiSettings, (patch: Partial) => void] ``` wrapping `loadKeatingUiSettings` + `subscribeKeatingUiSettings` + `saveKeatingUiSettings`, adopted by `ProvidersModelsTab` and `KeatingUiSettingsTab` only. Low-risk, ~30 lines, kills the duplicated load/subscribe boilerplate. Everything else (persona.ts, speech.ts, proxy SettingsStore) stays as-is. **Tests:** extend `web/src/test/ui-settings.test.ts` with a subscribe-propagation test (save → subscriber invoked with merged settings); add `use-ui-settings.test.ts` if the hook is added (vitest + happy-dom, render via existing test patterns). --- ## Part 2 — Unified adaptive SessionBrowser ### Breakpoint decision: **1024px**, single source of truth Justification: the desktop sidebar needs 288–640px (`SIDEBAR_MIN_W/MAX_W`) alongside a usable chat column; at 768–1023px that squeezes chat below ~450px. `SessionSidebar` already uses 1024 (`MD_BREAKPOINT`); the 768px side-panel mode only exists in the dialog being deleted, so standardizing on 1024 is also the minimal-churn choice. Export it once: `export const SESSION_BROWSER_BREAKPOINT = 1024` from the new component (or `session-tree.ts` sibling module) and use a single `useMediaQuery`-style hook. ### Phase 2.1 — `useSessions()` shared data hook **Created:** `web/src/hooks/use-sessions.ts` ```ts export interface UseSessionsResult { items: SessionMetadata[]; loading: boolean; query: string; setQuery: (q: string) => void; /** non-null while searching: flat, recency-sorted, capped */ flatResults: SessionMetadata[] | null; /** tree roots when not searching */ roots: SessionTreeNode[]; heroes: Map; // empty Map unless withHeroes reload: () => Promise; rename: (id: string, title: string, fromAi?: boolean) => Promise; // updateSessionTitle + dispatch keating:sessions-changed remove: (id: string) => Promise; // sessions.deleteSession + dispatch error: string | null; } export function useSessions(opts?: { withHeroes?: boolean; flatLimit?: number }): UseSessionsResult ``` - Encapsulates: `sessions.getAllMetadata()`, `buildArtifactHeroMap(keatingStorage)` (only when `withHeroes`, catch → empty Map, matching `SessionCardGrid.tsx:76-79`), the `keating:sessions-changed` listener, the search filter (union of the three components' current predicates: title/preview/relative-date/messageCount), and `buildSessionTree`. Commit Phase 2.1 alone (pure addition, no behavior change). ### Phase 2.2 — `SessionBrowser.tsx` (adaptive shell) + desktop CRUD parity **Created:** `web/src/components/SessionBrowser.tsx` ```ts export interface SessionBrowserProps { activeSessionId?: string; forkingSessionId?: string | null; forkedSessionId?: string | null; collapsed?: boolean; // desktop only onCollapsedChange?: (next: boolean) => void; mobileOpen?: boolean; // <1024 full-screen sheet visibility onMobileClose?: () => void; onLoad: (sessionId: string) => void | Promise; onFork: (sessionId: string) => void | Promise; onNewSession?: () => void; onSuggestTitle?: (sessionId: string) => Promise; // AI title, threaded to both variants } ``` Structure: - Top-level component owns `useSessions({ withHeroes: !isDesktop })` and the breakpoint media query, then renders `` or `` (both in the same file or as `SessionBrowser/` siblings — prefer two files `SessionBrowserDesktop.tsx` / `SessionBrowserSheet.tsx` given SessionSidebar is already 434 lines). - **Desktop panel**: lift the current `SessionSidebar` desktop code verbatim (collapsed strip, resizable width incl. `keating_session_sidebar_width`, tree with `keating:session-tree-collapsed` persistence, fork buttons) minus its data fetching (now from the hook) and minus the "Manage" button. Add per-row CRUD to reach parity with the deleted dialog: a hover-revealed action cluster (Fork — existing; Sparkles → `onSuggestTitle` flowing into inline rename input prefilled; Pencil → inline rename, enabled per the existing `aiGeneratedTitle` gate; Trash → inline confirm strip). Port the rename/delete/suggest state machine from `SessionManagerDialog.tsx:101-181` (busy/error/pendingDelete/aiSuggestion states) — this logic moves, it is not rewritten. To keep rows uncluttered, collapse the four actions behind a `MoreHorizontal` popover on rows, with Fork remaining a first-class button (current behavior). - **Mobile sheet**: lift `SessionCardGrid` wholesale (it is already a full-screen `fixed inset-0` sheet with backdrop, masonry, `SessionCard` + `ForkMapCard`, and rename/delete). Changes: remove the "Manage" button; add a Sparkles action to `SessionCard`'s action row wired to `onSuggestTitle` (prefill its existing rename input); route rename/delete through the hook's `rename`/`remove` instead of its local `updateSessionTitle`/`deleteSession` calls; keep the `role="dialog"` avoidance comment/behavior (app.css full-width-button rule). Add `pb-[env(safe-area-inset-bottom)]` on the scroller if not already covered by app.css. **Modified:** `web/src/components/SessionCard.tsx` — optional `onSuggestTitle?: (id: string) => Promise` prop; when present, show Sparkles button that awaits it and enters rename mode with the draft. `ForkMapCard` threading is optional (its rows can omit suggest; note as stretch if its API makes it noisy). Commit Phase 2.2 with `SessionSidebar`/`SessionCardGrid` still present (SessionBrowser not yet wired) — reviewable as pure addition, or alternatively fold 2.2+2.3 into one commit if dead-code-in-tree between commits is unwanted. Recommended: keep them separate but wire in the same PR. ### Phase 2.3 — Wire in, delete the old trio **Modified:** - `web/src/hooks/useKeatingAgent.tsx`: - Replace `sessionSidebarElement` (:794-812) with `` (same props minus `onOpenSessions`, plus `onSuggestTitle`). - Delete `sessionManagerDialogElement` (:773-792), the `SessionManagerDialog` import (:11), and `sessionsDialog = useDialogState()` (:182). - Repoint `openSessions` (:769-771, exported at :119/:971) instead of deleting it — keep the public API stable: on <1024 it calls the store's `toggleMobileSidebar`/open action; on desktop it calls `setSidebarCollapsed(false)`. All persisted keys survive unchanged: `keating_session_sidebar_width`, `keating:session-tree-collapsed`, `keating:session-sidebar-collapsed`. No session-data schema changes. **Tests:** update/keep `session-fork.test.ts`, `fork-map-layout.test.ts`, `session-card-visuals.test.ts` (should pass untouched — logic modules unchanged). Add `use-sessions.test.ts` (Phase 2.1). If any test imports `SessionCardGrid`/`SessionSidebar` (grep before deleting), update imports to SessionBrowser internals. --- ## Ordering & commits 1. **Commit 1:** Phase 1.1 — SettingsDialog chips. 2. **Commit 2:** Phase 1.2 — grouped tabs (LearningTab, ModelsProvidersTab, nav merges, tab registration). 3. **Commit 3:** Phase 1.3 — subscribe fix (+ optional `useKeatingUiSettings`) + test. 4. **Commit 4:** Phase 2.1 — `useSessions` hook + test. 5. **Commit 5:** Phases 2.2 + 2.3 — SessionBrowser, wiring, deletions (single commit so the tree never ships two parallel session UIs; the diff is large but mostly moved code — note "moved from SessionSidebar/SessionCardGrid" in the message). You are designing an implementation plan for a refactor of the keating web app at /home/diogenes/Projects/keating (React + Tailwind v4 + Vite, uses bun). Read the key files yourself to validate. Do NOT write any files — return the plan as your final message.

## Goal 1: Simplified Settings — consolidate 5 tabs into ~3 grouped tabs

Current state (verified by exploration):
- `web/src/components/SettingsDialog.tsx` (101 lines) — generic shell taking `tabs: SettingsTabDef[]`, desktop left sidebar + mobile `<select>` picker.
- Tabs assembled in `web/src/hooks/useKeatingAgent.tsx:816-828`: ProvidersModelsTab (973 lines: Cloud Providers/keys, Web Search, Provider Visibility, My Models, Custom Providers), TeacherPersonaTab (102), SpeechSettingsTab (363), KeatingUiSettingsTab (398: Chat Interface, Share Links, Animation Renderer, Reasoning Level, Image generation), ProxyTab (60).
- Persistence fragmented: localStorage keys `keating_ui_settings` (ui-settings.ts, event `keating:ui-settings-changed`), `keating:teacher-persona` (persona.ts), `keating:web:speech` (speech.ts); IndexedDB pi-web-ui SettingsStore for `proxy.*`; providerKeys/customProviders stores. `hooks/keating-storage.ts` wires backends (IndexedDB or P2P).
- Known issues: Interface-owned `keating_ui_settings` blob backs Providers-tab UI (customModels, hiddenProviders, recentModels); two overlapping toggles `googleGrounding` vs `webSearch`; ProvidersModelsTab manually reloads via `setSettings(loadKeatingUiSettings())` (:161,191,471) instead of `subscribeKeatingUiSettings`; SettingsSectionNav used in Providers+Speech but not Interface; shared `SettingRow.tsx` and `Toggle.tsx` exist.
- Mobile: app.css :402-416 makes all `[role=dialog]` buttons 100% width below 768px unless `dialog-compact-button`/`dialog-icon-button`; sidebar hidden below `sm` replaced with `<select>`.

User-approved direction: **3 grouped tabs** — e.g. "Models & Providers" (keys, visibility, custom models, web search, proxy?), "Learning" (persona, speech), "App" (interface, share links, animation, reasoning, image gen, proxy). Mobile: tabs become segmented chips at top instead of a `<select>`. Decide the exact grouping and justify. Keep tab content composed from the existing section components where practical (extract sections rather than rewrite). Recommend whether to unify persistence now (e.g. a single `useKeatingSetting` hook wrapping the existing keys) or defer — bias toward low-risk incremental.

## Goal 2: Unified adaptive SessionBrowser

Current state:
- Three parallel UIs off one `sessions.getAllMetadata()` source: `web/src/components/SessionSidebar.tsx` (434, desktop resizable tree sidebar, ≥1024px; below that delegates to SessionCardGrid at lines 215-229), `SessionCardGrid.tsx` (252, mobile masonry card grid with SessionCard + ForkMapCard), `SessionManagerDialog.tsx` (363, modal <768px / left side panel ≥768px, full CRUD: load, fork, AI title suggest, rename gated on aiGeneratedTitle, delete-with-confirm).
- Each keeps its own items state, search box, and `keating:sessions-changed` reload listener; each calls `buildSessionTree`/`flattenSessionTree` from `session-tree.ts` (75).
- Orchestrated by `web/src/hooks/useKeatingAgent.tsx`: builds sessionManagerDialogElement (:773-792) and sessionSidebarElement (:794-812); ops newSession (:581), shareSession (:605), loadSession (:620), forkSession (:666), title suggestion (:720). `Chat.tsx` renders `{sessionSidebar}` at :482 and `{dialogs}` at :555.
- Mobile drawer state in `web/src/stores/keating-agent-store.ts`: mobileSidebarOpen etc; desktop collapse persisted at `keating:session-sidebar-collapsed`.
- Breakpoint mismatch: dialog side-panel at ≥768, sidebar at ≥1024.

User-approved direction: **one adaptive `SessionBrowser` component** — desktop: left sidebar (keep resizable + tree + collapse); mobile: full-screen sheet with the card-grid visuals; delete `SessionManagerDialog` entirely and fold its CRUD (rename, delete-confirm, AI-title) into SessionBrowser; single shared data hook (e.g. `useSessions()` encapsulating items/search/tree/reload-on-`keating:sessions-changed`). Unify on one breakpoint (1024px? justify). Preserve fork tree, ForkMapCard, SharedSession flow, P2P backend compatibility.

## Constraints
- Incremental, reviewable steps; keep git history sane.
- Reuse: SettingRow, Toggle, SettingsSectionNav, session-tree.ts, session-card-visuals.ts, existing keating-agent-store.
- Mobile must use screen real estate well (full-screen sheet, safe-area, tap targets already handled in app.css).
- Tests exist at web/src/test/ (e.g. ui-settings.test.ts); note what tests to add/update.
- There are uncommitted changes in the working tree (p2p/electron work in progress) — plan must not assume clean tree but shouldn't touch desktop/ or packages/p2p-core.

## Deliverable
A step-by-step implementation plan: ordered phases, files created/modified/deleted per phase, key component APIs (props/hooks signatures), migration notes for any storage changes, risks, and a verification checklist. Also include a short "stretch goals" addendum list (things you notice worth improving but out of scope — e.g. the googleGrounding/webSearch merge, ProvidersModelsTab decomposition, persistence unification if deferred).

# Implementation Plan: Settings Consolidation + Unified SessionBrowser I validated the codebase state described in the brief by reading the key files. Notable findings that shape the plan: - `SessionCard.tsx` **already implements** inline rename (Pencil), delete-with-confirm (Trash + `confirmingDelete`), via `onRename`/`onDelete` props — the mobile grid already has most of the CRUD that `SessionManagerDialog` provides. The only capability the dialog exclusively owns is **AI title suggestion** (`onSuggestTitle`) and the desktop-visible rename/delete (the desktop `SessionSidebar` rows have neither). - `openSessions`/`sessionsDialog` have no consumers outside `useKeatingAgent.tsx` (grep confirmed), so removing the dialog is contained to that hook plus the two "Manage" buttons in `SessionSidebar.tsx:289` and `SessionCardGrid.tsx:145`. - `SettingsDialog.tsx` already renders the mobile `` (lines 76-85) with a horizontal chip row: `sm:hidden flex gap-1.5 overflow-x-auto` of pill buttons (same active styling as the sidebar buttons, `whitespace-nowrap`, `min-h-9` for tap targets). With 3 tabs, chips fit a 320px viewport; `overflow-x-auto` covers safety. Keep the desktop sidebar unchanged. - Add `defaultTabId?: string` prop (optional, future-proofing deep links like "open proxy settings"); keep index-based state internally, resolve id → index once. - Chips are plain buttons < 100% width — they're inside `[role=dialog]`, so per `app.css:402-416` they'd stretch on mobile; give them the existing `dialog-compact-button` class to opt out (reuse, no CSS change). ### Phase 1.2 — Section-nav prefix collision guard + composed tabs **Created:** - `web/src/components/settings/LearningTab.tsx` (~40 lines): ```tsx export function LearningTab({ onSpeechSettingsChange }: { onSpeechSettingsChange: (s: SpeechSettings) => void }) { // //

Teacher Persona

// } ``` Speech already has its own `SettingsSectionNav` with `settings-section-speech-*` ids (`SpeechSettingsTab.tsx:136`). Add an optional `hideNav?: boolean` prop to `SpeechSettingsTab` so LearningTab renders one merged nav (persona + the 5 speech section entries) instead of two stacked navs. TeacherPersonaTab (102 lines) gets wrapped in a section div with a heading — no internal changes. - `web/src/components/settings/ModelsProvidersTab.tsx` (~30 lines): renders `` … simpler: renders `` followed by a `#settings-section-proxy` section wrapping ``. Add an optional `extraNavSections?: SectionDef[]` prop to `ProvidersModelsTab` so its existing nav (`ProvidersModelsTab` nav block around line 267) includes the Proxy entry. ProxyTab (60 lines) is unchanged. **Modified:** - `web/src/hooks/useKeatingAgent.tsx:816-828` — tabs array becomes: ```tsx tabs={[ { id: "models", label: "Models & Providers", component: }, { id: "learning", label: "Learning", component: }, { id: "app", label: "App", component: }, ]} ``` - `web/src/components/SpeechSettingsTab.tsx` — `hideNav` prop + export its `SECTIONS` const. - `web/src/components/ProvidersModelsTab.tsx` — `extraNavSections` prop (nav array spread only; ~3-line diff). - `web/src/components/KeatingUiSettingsTab.tsx` — add a `SettingsSectionNav` (Interface / Share Links / Animation / Reasoning / Images) for parity, wrapping its existing groups in `id="settings-section-*"` divs with `scroll-mt-20`. Purely additive. Old tab files are **not deleted** — `TeacherPersonaTab`, `SpeechSettingsTab`, `ProxyTab`, `ProvidersModelsTab` remain as the section building blocks; only their top-level registration moves. ### Phase 1.3 — Persistence: minimal fix now, unification deferred **Recommendation: do not unify persistence in this refactor.** The three localStorage keys + IndexedDB SettingsStore each have working subscribe/migration paths and P2P backend wiring in `keating-storage.ts`; merging them risks data migration bugs for zero UI payoff. Do only the known-bug fix: **Modified:** `web/src/components/ProvidersModelsTab.tsx` — replace the three manual `setSettings(loadKeatingUiSettings())` reload sites (:161, :191, :471) with a single `useEffect` that calls `subscribeKeatingUiSettings(setSettings)`. This fixes stale state when the App tab (or another window) mutates `keating_ui_settings` while the Providers tab is mounted — newly relevant because grouped tabs make cross-tab edits within one dialog session more likely. Optionally introduce `web/src/hooks/use-ui-settings.ts`: ```ts export function useKeatingUiSettings(): [KeatingUiSettings, (patch: Partial) => void] ``` wrapping `loadKeatingUiSettings` + `subscribeKeatingUiSettings` + `saveKeatingUiSettings`, adopted by `ProvidersModelsTab` and `KeatingUiSettingsTab` only. Low-risk, ~30 lines, kills the duplicated load/subscribe boilerplate. Everything else (persona.ts, speech.ts, proxy SettingsStore) stays as-is. **Tests:** extend `web/src/test/ui-settings.test.ts` with a subscribe-propagation test (save → subscriber invoked with merged settings); add `use-ui-settings.test.ts` if the hook is added (vitest + happy-dom, render via existing test patterns). --- ## Part 2 — Unified adaptive SessionBrowser ### Breakpoint decision: **1024px**, single source of truth Justification: the desktop sidebar needs 288–640px (`SIDEBAR_MIN_W/MAX_W`) alongside a usable chat column; at 768–1023px that squeezes chat below ~450px. `SessionSidebar` already uses 1024 (`MD_BREAKPOINT`); the 768px side-panel mode only exists in the dialog being deleted, so standardizing on 1024 is also the minimal-churn choice. Export it once: `export const SESSION_BROWSER_BREAKPOINT = 1024` from the new component (or `session-tree.ts` sibling module) and use a single `useMediaQuery`-style hook. ### Phase 2.1 — `useSessions()` shared data hook **Created:** `web/src/hooks/use-sessions.ts` ```ts export interface UseSessionsResult { items: SessionMetadata[]; loading: boolean; query: string; setQuery: (q: string) => void; /** non-null while searching: flat, recency-sorted, capped */ flatResults: SessionMetadata[] | null; /** tree roots when not searching */ roots: SessionTreeNode[]; heroes: Map; // empty Map unless withHeroes reload: () => Promise; rename: (id: string, title: string, fromAi?: boolean) => Promise; // updateSessionTitle + dispatch keating:sessions-changed remove: (id: string) => Promise; // sessions.deleteSession + dispatch error: string | null; } export function useSessions(opts?: { withHeroes?: boolean; flatLimit?: number }): UseSessionsResult ``` - Encapsulates: `sessions.getAllMetadata()`, `buildArtifactHeroMap(keatingStorage)` (only when `withHeroes`, catch → empty Map, matching `SessionCardGrid.tsx:76-79`), the `keating:sessions-changed` listener, the search filter (union of the three components' current predicates: title/preview/relative-date/messageCount), and `buildSessionTree`. SharedSession share-link flow unchanged (`shareLinkMode` still read from ui-settings); works with P2P backend enabled; `bunx vitest` green; no console errors at 375px, 768px, 1024px, 1440px widths. ## Stretch goals (out of scope, noticed while exploring) - Merge `googleGrounding` + `webSearch` into one setting with a one-time migration in `normalizeUiSettings`. - Decompose the 973-line `ProvidersModelsTab.tsx` into per-section files under `components/settings/`. - Full persistence unification: a `useKeatingSetting(key)` facade over localStorage keys + SettingsStore, enabling P2P sync of persona/speech/ui settings (currently only proxy/keys go through SettingsStore). - Move `customModels`/`hiddenProviders`/`recentModels` out of the Interface-owned `keating_ui_settings` blob into a provider-scoped store. - `ForkMapCard` AI-suggest support; keyboard navigation for the session tree (`role="tree"` exists but no arrow-key handling). - Deep-link support `?settings=proxy` using the new `defaultTabId` prop. - Replace ad-hoc `window.matchMedia` copies with one shared `useMediaQuery` hook (three implementations exist today). ### Critical Files for Implementation - /home/diogenes/Projects/keating/web/src/hooks/useKeatingAgent.tsx - /home/diogenes/Projects/keating/web/src/components/SessionSidebar.tsx - /home/diogenes/Projects/keating/web/src/components/SessionCardGrid.tsx - /home/diogenes/Projects/keating/web/src/components/SettingsDialog.tsx - /home/diogenes/Projects/keating/web/src/components/SessionManagerDialog.tsx
