<result># Implementation Plan: Settings Consolidation + Unified SessionBrowser

I validated the codebase state described in the brief by reading the key files. Notable findings that shape the plan:

- `SessionCard.tsx` **already implements** inline rename (Pencil), delete-with-confirm (Trash + `confirmingDelete`), via `onRename`/`onDelete` props — the mobile grid already has most of the CRUD that `SessionManagerDialog` provides. The only capability the dialog exclusively owns is **AI title suggestion** (`onSuggestTitle`) and the desktop-visible rename/delete (the desktop `SessionSidebar` rows have neither).
- `openSessions`/`sessionsDialog` have no consumers outside `useKeatingAgent.tsx` (grep confirmed), so removing the dialog is contained to that hook plus the two "Manage" buttons in `SessionSidebar.tsx:289` and `SessionCardGrid.tsx:145`.
- `SettingsDialog.tsx` already renders the mobile `<select>` unconditionally in the header row (visible when the `hidden sm:flex` sidebar is hidden) — swapping to chips is a small, isolated change.
- `SessionCardGrid` fetches artifact heroes (`buildArtifactHeroMap`) in the same reload as metadata; the shared hook must support that.
- Uncommitted changes touch `Chat.tsx`, `useKeatingAgent.tsx` adjacents (`keating-storage.ts`, `App.tsx`, `app.css`) — plan works on top of the current tree; commits should be scoped per phase so p2p WIP stays separable.

---

## Part 1 — Settings: 5 tabs → 3 grouped tabs

### Grouping decision (and justification)

| Tab | Contents | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| **Models & Providers** | Cloud provider keys, Web Search, Provider Visibility, My Models, Custom Providers, **Proxy** | Everything about *how the app reaches LLMs*. Proxy belongs here, not "App": it exists to route provider requests (CORS/relay); a user debugging a failing provider key will look for proxy config next to keys. Keeps the 973-line ProvidersModelsTab untouched. |
| **Learning** | Teacher Persona, Speech & Voice | Both configure the *tutoring experience* (who the teacher is, how it speaks). Natural pair; both are small components already. |
| **App** | Chat Interface toggles, Share Links, Animation Renderer, Reasoning Level, Image Generation, Font | Exactly the current KeatingUiSettingsTab. Reasoning level stays here rather than Models & Providers because it is a chat-behavior default persisted in `keating_ui_settings`, and moving it would either split the Interface tab or drag `ui-settings` state into ProvidersModelsTab — churn with no user benefit. |

This grouping requires **zero decomposition** of existing tab components: the two merged tabs are composed by concatenation, with section navs merged.

### Phase 1.1 — SettingsDialog mobile chips (isolated, ship first)

**Modified:** `web/src/components/SettingsDialog.tsx`

- Replace the header `<select>` (lines 76-85) with a horizontal chip row: `sm:hidden flex gap-1.5 overflow-x-auto` of pill buttons (same active styling as the sidebar buttons, `whitespace-nowrap`, `min-h-9` for tap targets). With 3 tabs, chips fit a 320px viewport; `overflow-x-auto` covers safety. Keep the desktop sidebar unchanged.
- Add `defaultTabId?: string` prop (optional, future-proofing deep links like "open proxy settings"); keep index-based state internally, resolve id → index once.
- Chips are plain buttons < 100% width — they're inside `[role=dialog]`, so per `app.css:402-416` they'd stretch on mobile; give them the existing `dialog-compact-button` class to opt out (reuse, no CSS change).

### Phase 1.2 — Section-nav prefix collision guard + composed tabs

**Created:**
- `web/src/components/settings/LearningTab.tsx` (~40 lines):
  ```tsx
  export function LearningTab({ onSpeechSettingsChange }: { onSpeechSettingsChange: (s: SpeechSettings) => void }) {
    // <SettingsSectionNav sections={[{id:"persona",...},{id:"speech-enable",...}, ...speech sections]}/>
    // <section id="settings-section-persona"><h3>Teacher Persona</h3><TeacherPersonaTab/></section>
    // <SpeechSettingsTab onSettingsChange={...} hideNav />
  }
  ```
  Speech already has its own `SettingsSectionNav` with `settings-section-speech-*` ids (`SpeechSettingsTab.tsx:136`). Add an optional `hideNav?: boolean` prop to `SpeechSettingsTab` so LearningTab renders one merged nav (persona + the 5 speech section entries) instead of two stacked navs. TeacherPersonaTab (102 lines) gets wrapped in a section div with a heading — no internal changes.
- `web/src/components/settings/ModelsProvidersTab.tsx` (~30 lines): renders `<ProvidersModelsTab extraSections={[{id:"proxy", label:"Proxy"}]} />` … simpler: renders `<ProvidersModelsTab />` followed by a `#settings-section-proxy` section wrapping `<ProxyTab />`. Add an optional `extraNavSections?: SectionDef[]` prop to `ProvidersModelsTab` so its existing nav (`ProvidersModelsTab` nav block around line 267) includes the Proxy entry. ProxyTab (60 lines) is unchanged.

**Modified:**
- `web/src/hooks/useKeatingAgent.tsx:816-828` — tabs array becomes:
  ```tsx
  tabs={[
    { id: "models", label: "Models & Providers", component: <ModelsProvidersTab /> },
    { id: "learning", label: "Learning", component: <LearningTab onSpeechSettingsChange={setSpeechSettings} /> },
    { id: "app", label: "App", component: <KeatingUiSettingsTab /> },
  ]}
  ```
- `web/src/components/SpeechSettingsTab.tsx` — `hideNav` prop + export its `SECTIONS` const.
- `web/src/components/ProvidersModelsTab.tsx` — `extraNavSections` prop (nav array spread only; ~3-line diff).
- `web/src/components/KeatingUiSettingsTab.tsx` — add a `SettingsSectionNav` (Interface / Share Links / Animation / Reasoning / Images) for parity, wrapping its existing groups in `id="settings-section-*"` divs with `scroll-mt-20`. Purely additive.

Old tab files are **not deleted** — `TeacherPersonaTab`, `SpeechSettingsTab`, `ProxyTab`, `ProvidersModelsTab` remain as the section building blocks; only their top-level registration moves.

### Phase 1.3 — Persistence: minimal fix now, unification deferred

**Recommendation: do not unify persistence in this refactor.** The three localStorage keys + IndexedDB SettingsStore each have working subscribe/migration paths and P2P backend wiring in `keating-storage.ts`; merging them risks data migration bugs for zero UI payoff. Do only the known-bug fix:

**Modified:** `web/src/components/ProvidersModelsTab.tsx` — replace the three manual `setSettings(loadKeatingUiSettings())` reload sites (:161, :191, :471) with a single `useEffect` that calls `subscribeKeatingUiSettings(setSettings)`. This fixes stale state when the App tab (or another window) mutates `keating_ui_settings` while the Providers tab is mounted — newly relevant because grouped tabs make cross-tab edits within one dialog session more likely.

Optionally introduce `web/src/hooks/use-ui-settings.ts`:
```ts
export function useKeatingUiSettings(): [KeatingUiSettings, (patch: Partial<KeatingUiSettings>) => void]
```
wrapping `loadKeatingUiSettings` + `subscribeKeatingUiSettings` + `saveKeatingUiSettings`, adopted by `ProvidersModelsTab` and `KeatingUiSettingsTab` only. Low-risk, ~30 lines, kills the duplicated load/subscribe boilerplate. Everything else (persona.ts, speech.ts, proxy SettingsStore) stays as-is.

**Tests:** extend `web/src/test/ui-settings.test.ts` with a subscribe-propagation test (save → subscriber invoked with merged settings); add `use-ui-settings.test.ts` if the hook is added (vitest + happy-dom, render via existing test patterns).

---

## Part 2 — Unified adaptive SessionBrowser

### Breakpoint decision: **1024px**, single source of truth

Justification: the desktop sidebar needs 288–640px (`SIDEBAR_MIN_W/MAX_W`) alongside a usable chat column; at 768–1023px that squeezes chat below ~450px. `SessionSidebar` already uses 1024 (`MD_BREAKPOINT`); the 768px side-panel mode only exists in the dialog being deleted, so standardizing on 1024 is also the minimal-churn choice. Export it once: `export const SESSION_BROWSER_BREAKPOINT = 1024` from the new component (or `session-tree.ts` sibling module) and use a single `useMediaQuery`-style hook.

### Phase 2.1 — `useSessions()` shared data hook

**Created:** `web/src/hooks/use-sessions.ts`

```ts
export interface UseSessionsResult {
  items: SessionMetadata[];
  loading: boolean;
  query: string;
  setQuery: (q: string) => void;
  /** non-null while searching: flat, recency-sorted, capped */
  flatResults: SessionMetadata[] | null;
  /** tree roots when not searching */
  roots: SessionTreeNode[];
  heroes: Map<string, ArtifactHero>;        // empty Map unless withHeroes
  reload: () => Promise<void>;
  rename: (id: string, title: string, fromAi?: boolean) => Promise<void>; // updateSessionTitle + dispatch keating:sessions-changed
  remove: (id: string) => Promise<void>;    // sessions.deleteSession + dispatch
  error: string | null;
}
export function useSessions(opts?: { withHeroes?: boolean; flatLimit?: number }): UseSessionsResult
```

- Encapsulates: `sessions.getAllMetadata()`, `buildArtifactHeroMap(keatingStorage)` (only when `withHeroes`, catch → empty Map, matching `SessionCardGrid.tsx:76-79`), the `keating:sessions-changed` listener, the search filter (union of the three components' current predicates: title/preview/relative-date/messageCount), and `buildSessionTree`. Flattening stays in the consumer because collapsed-node state is desktop-only.
- `rename`/`remove` dispatch `keating:sessions-changed` themselves — this replaces the dialog's `onDeleted`/`onRenamed` callback plumbing in `useKeatingAgent.tsx:779-784` (which existed only to fire that same event).
- P2P compatibility: the hook only talks to the `sessions` facade from `keating-storage.ts`, same as today — backend-agnostic by construction.

**Tests:** `web/src/test/use-sessions.test.ts` — filter predicate + tree/flat switching + event-driven reload (mock the `sessions` module; the repo already mocks storage in `session-fork.test.ts`).

Commit Phase 2.1 alone (pure addition, no behavior change).

### Phase 2.2 — `SessionBrowser.tsx` (adaptive shell) + desktop CRUD parity

**Created:** `web/src/components/SessionBrowser.tsx`

```ts
export interface SessionBrowserProps {
  activeSessionId?: string;
  forkingSessionId?: string | null;
  forkedSessionId?: string | null;
  collapsed?: boolean;                       // desktop only
  onCollapsedChange?: (next: boolean) => void;
  mobileOpen?: boolean;                      // <1024 full-screen sheet visibility
  onMobileClose?: () => void;
  onLoad: (sessionId: string) => void | Promise<void>;
  onFork: (sessionId: string) => void | Promise<void>;
  onNewSession?: () => void;
  onSuggestTitle?: (sessionId: string) => Promise<string>;  // AI title, threaded to both variants
}
```

Structure:
- Top-level component owns `useSessions({ withHeroes: !isDesktop })` and the breakpoint media query, then renders `<DesktopSessionPanel>` or `<MobileSessionSheet>` (both in the same file or as `SessionBrowser/` siblings — prefer two files `SessionBrowserDesktop.tsx` / `SessionBrowserSheet.tsx` given SessionSidebar is already 434 lines).
- **Desktop panel**: lift the current `SessionSidebar` desktop code verbatim (collapsed strip, resizable width incl. `keating_session_sidebar_width`, tree with `keating:session-tree-collapsed` persistence, fork buttons) minus its data fetching (now from the hook) and minus the "Manage" button. Add per-row CRUD to reach parity with the deleted dialog: a hover-revealed action cluster (Fork — existing; Sparkles → `onSuggestTitle` flowing into inline rename input prefilled; Pencil → inline rename, enabled per the existing `aiGeneratedTitle` gate; Trash → inline confirm strip). Port the rename/delete/suggest state machine from `SessionManagerDialog.tsx:101-181` (busy/error/pendingDelete/aiSuggestion states) — this logic moves, it is not rewritten. To keep rows uncluttered, collapse the four actions behind a `MoreHorizontal` popover on rows, with Fork remaining a first-class button (current behavior).
- **Mobile sheet**: lift `SessionCardGrid` wholesale (it is already a full-screen `fixed inset-0` sheet with backdrop, masonry, `SessionCard` + `ForkMapCard`, and rename/delete). Changes: remove the "Manage" button; add a Sparkles action to `SessionCard`'s action row wired to `onSuggestTitle` (prefill its existing rename input); route rename/delete through the hook's `rename`/`remove` instead of its local `updateSessionTitle`/`deleteSession` calls; keep the `role="dialog"` avoidance comment/behavior (app.css full-width-button rule). Add `pb-[env(safe-area-inset-bottom)]` on the scroller if not already covered by app.css.

**Modified:** `web/src/components/SessionCard.tsx` — optional `onSuggestTitle?: (id: string) => Promise<string>` prop; when present, show Sparkles button that awaits it and enters rename mode with the draft. `ForkMapCard` threading is optional (its rows can omit suggest; note as stretch if its API makes it noisy).

Commit Phase 2.2 with `SessionSidebar`/`SessionCardGrid` still present (SessionBrowser not yet wired) — reviewable as pure addition, or alternatively fold 2.2+2.3 into one commit if dead-code-in-tree between commits is unwanted. Recommended: keep them separate but wire in the same PR.

### Phase 2.3 — Wire in, delete the old trio

**Modified:**
- `web/src/hooks/useKeatingAgent.tsx`:
  - Replace `sessionSidebarElement` (:794-812) with `<SessionBrowser ... onSuggestTitle={suggestSessionTitle} />` (same props minus `onOpenSessions`, plus `onSuggestTitle`).
  - Delete `sessionManagerDialogElement` (:773-792), the `SessionManagerDialog` import (:11), and `sessionsDialog = useDialogState()` (:182).
  - Repoint `openSessions` (:769-771, exported at :119/:971) instead of deleting it — keep the public API stable: on <1024 it calls the store's `toggleMobileSidebar`/open action; on desktop it calls `setSidebarCollapsed(false)`. (No external consumers found, but the hook's return type is public surface; cheap to preserve.)
- `web/src/pages/Chat.tsx` — remove the dialog element from the `{dialogs}` fragment if listed individually; `{sessionSidebar}` render point (:482) unchanged (rename the variable to `sessionBrowser` for clarity).
- `web/src/stores/keating-agent-store.ts` — no changes needed: `mobileSidebarOpen` and `keating:session-sidebar-collapsed` persistence are reused as-is.

**Deleted:**
- `web/src/components/SessionManagerDialog.tsx`
- `web/src/components/SessionSidebar.tsx` (logic relocated into SessionBrowser desktop variant)
- `web/src/components/SessionCardGrid.tsx` (relocated into sheet variant)
- Remove now-dead `session-manager-dialog` / `session-manager-side-panel` CSS classes from `web/src/app.css` (careful: app.css has uncommitted p2p edits — make surgical removals, don't reformat).

**Migration notes (storage):** none required. All persisted keys survive unchanged: `keating_session_sidebar_width`, `keating:session-tree-collapsed`, `keating:session-sidebar-collapsed`. No session-data schema changes.

**Tests:** update/keep `session-fork.test.ts`, `fork-map-layout.test.ts`, `session-card-visuals.test.ts` (should pass untouched — logic modules unchanged). Add `use-sessions.test.ts` (Phase 2.1). If any test imports `SessionCardGrid`/`SessionSidebar` (grep before deleting), update imports to SessionBrowser internals.

---

## Ordering & commits

1. **Commit 1:** Phase 1.1 — SettingsDialog chips.
2. **Commit 2:** Phase 1.2 — grouped tabs (LearningTab, ModelsProvidersTab, nav merges, tab registration).
3. **Commit 3:** Phase 1.3 — subscribe fix (+ optional `useKeatingUiSettings`) + test.
4. **Commit 4:** Phase 2.1 — `useSessions` hook + test.
5. **Commit 5:** Phases 2.2 + 2.3 — SessionBrowser, wiring, deletions (single commit so the tree never ships two parallel session UIs; the diff is large but mostly moved code — note "moved from SessionSidebar/SessionCardGrid" in the message).

Each commit leaves `bun run build` / `bunx vitest` green. Do not touch `desktop/` or `packages/p2p-core`; stage only the files listed (working tree has unrelated WIP in `Chat.tsx`/`app.css`/`useKeatingAgent.tsx` — coordinate or use `git add -p` equivalent staging per hunk).

## Risks

- **Uncommitted WIP overlap** in `useKeatingAgent.tsx`, `Chat.tsx`, `app.css`, `keating-storage.ts` — highest practical risk; rebase-style conflicts within one tree. Mitigation: land settings phases (low overlap) first; confirm p2p WIP status before Phase 2.3.
- **Desktop CRUD ergonomics**: adding rename/delete/suggest to dense tree rows can clutter; popover cluster mitigates. Verify tap targets ≥ 40px per app.css conventions.
- **aiGeneratedTitle rename gate**: the dialog disabled Pencil until an AI title existed (`SessionManagerDialog.tsx:294`), but `SessionCard` allows free rename. Unify on the *card* behavior (free rename, `updateSessionTitle(id, title, fromAi || session.aiGeneratedTitle)`) — the gate appears to be a product quirk, flag in PR for confirmation.
- **`role="dialog"` button-stretch rule** (`app.css:402-416`): the sheet must keep the non-dialog-role workaround; the desktop panel is an `<aside>` (unaffected); SettingsDialog chips need `dialog-compact-button`.
- **Hero map cost on desktop**: `withHeroes` gated to mobile keeps the desktop sidebar reload cheap (current behavior).
- **P2P/cloud backends**: all access stays behind `sessions`/`keatingStorage` facades; no direct IndexedDB in new code.

## Verification checklist

- Settings: 3 tabs on desktop sidebar and as mobile chips (<640px); chip row scrolls if cramped; section nav jump-links work in all three tabs; provider key add/remove, custom model add, proxy save, persona save, speech test, and every App toggle still persist across reload; editing App-tab settings while Models tab mounted reflects immediately (subscribe fix).
- Sessions desktop (≥1024): resize persists, collapse strip persists, tree expand/collapse persists, search flattens, load/fork/new work, rename/delete/AI-suggest work inline, fork animation classes (`session-fork-arrive`, "Forked" chip) intact.
- Sessions mobile (<1024): hamburger opens full-screen sheet; masonry with SessionCard heroes + ForkMapCard for forked roots; rename/delete/fork/suggest on cards; close on load; safe-area respected; no stretched buttons.
- Cross-cutting: fork from active session captures latest messages (snapshot-before-fork path unchanged); SharedSession share-link flow unchanged (`shareLinkMode` still read from ui-settings); works with P2P backend enabled; `bunx vitest` green; no console errors at 375px, 768px, 1024px, 1440px widths.

## Stretch goals (out of scope, noticed while exploring)

- Merge `googleGrounding` + `webSearch` into one setting with a one-time migration in `normalizeUiSettings`.
- Decompose the 973-line `ProvidersModelsTab.tsx` into per-section files under `components/settings/`.
- Full persistence unification: a `useKeatingSetting(key)` facade over localStorage keys + SettingsStore, enabling P2P sync of persona/speech/ui settings (currently only proxy/keys go through SettingsStore).
- Move `customModels`/`hiddenProviders`/`recentModels` out of the Interface-owned `keating_ui_settings` blob into a provider-scoped store.
- `ForkMapCard` AI-suggest support; keyboard navigation for the session tree (`role="tree"` exists but no arrow-key handling).
- Deep-link support `?settings=proxy` using the new `defaultTabId` prop.
- Replace ad-hoc `window.matchMedia` copies with one shared `useMediaQuery` hook (three implementations exist today).

### Critical Files for Implementation
- /home/diogenes/Projects/keating/web/src/hooks/useKeatingAgent.tsx
- /home/diogenes/Projects/keating/web/src/components/SessionSidebar.tsx
- /home/diogenes/Projects/keating/web/src/components/SessionCardGrid.tsx

input.content:
# Simplify Settings + Unified SessionBrowser

## Context

Two UX problems in the keating web app:

1. **Settings** are spread across 5 tabs (Providers & Models, Teacher Persona, Speech & Voice, Interface, Proxy) with fragmented persistence (3 direct localStorage keys + IndexedDB SettingsStore), cross-tab data ownership (`keating_ui_settings` blob backs the Providers UI), and a stale-state bug (ProvidersModelsTab manually reloads instead of subscribing).
2. **Sessions** have three independently-stateful list UIs off one data source: `SessionSidebar.tsx` (desktop tree, ≥1024px, embeds the grid below that), `SessionCardGrid.tsx` (mobile masonry sheet), and `SessionManagerDialog.tsx` (modal <768px / side panel ≥768px — overlapping the sidebar's breakpoint). Each duplicates items state, search, and `keating:sessions-changed` reload listeners.

User-approved direction: **3 grouped settings tabs** with mobile chip navigation, and **one adaptive SessionBrowser** (desktop sidebar / mobile full-screen sheet) replacing all three session UIs, with the Manage dialog deleted and its CRUD folded in.

Useful validated facts:
- `SessionCard.tsx` already has inline rename + delete-with-confirm; the dialog's only unique capability is AI title suggestion.
- `openSessions`/`sessionsDialog` have no consumers outside `useKeatingAgent.tsx`.
- Working tree has unrelated p2p/electron WIP in `Chat.tsx`, `useKeatingAgent.tsx`, `app.css`, `keating-storage.ts` — stage surgically per phase; never touch `desktop/` or `packages/p2p-core`.

---

## Part 1 — Settings: 5 tabs → 3

Grouping (composition only — no decomposition of existing tab components):

| Tab | Contents |
|---|---|
| **Models & Providers** | ProvidersModelsTab (keys, web search, visibility, custom models, custom providers) + **Proxy** section appended (proxy routes provider requests, so it belongs with keys) |
| **Learning** | Teacher Persona + Speech & Voice (both configure the tutoring experience) |
| **App** | KeatingUiSettingsTab as-is (chat interface, share links, animation renderer, reasoning level, image gen) |

### Phase 1.1 — Mobile chips in SettingsDialog
- `web/src/components/SettingsDialog.tsx`: replace the mobile `<select>` (lines ~76-85) with a horizontal chip row (`sm:hidden flex gap-1.5 overflow-x-auto`, pill buttons with active styling matching the sidebar, `min-h-9`). Chips need `dialog-compact-button` to escape the app.css:402-416 full-width dialog-button rule.
- Add optional `defaultTabId?: string` prop.

### Phase 1.2 — Composed tabs
- **New** `web/src/components/settings/ModelsProvidersTab.tsx` (~30 lines): `<ProvidersModelsTab extraNavSections={[{id:"proxy",label:"Proxy"}]} />` followed by a `#settings-section-proxy` section wrapping `<ProxyTab />` (unchanged).
- **New** `web/src/components/settings/LearningTab.tsx` (~40 lines): merged `SettingsSectionNav` (persona + speech sections), a persona section wrapping `<TeacherPersonaTab />`, then `<SpeechSettingsTab hideNav onSettingsChange={...} />`.
- **Modify** `SpeechSettingsTab.tsx`: add `hideNav?: boolean`, export its sections const.
- **Modify** `ProvidersModelsTab.tsx`: add `extraNavSections?` (spread into existing nav array, ~3 lines).
- **Modify** `KeatingUiSettingsTab.tsx`: add a `SettingsSectionNav` + `settings-section-*` ids for parity (purely additive).
- **Modify** `web/src/hooks/useKeatingAgent.tsx:816-828`: register the 3 tabs (models / learning / app).
- Old tab files stay as building blocks; only registration moves.

### Phase 1.3 — Persistence fix (unification deferred)
Do **not** merge storage mechanisms now (migration risk, no UI payoff). Only:
- Fix the stale-state bug: in `ProvidersModelsTab.tsx`, replace the three manual `setSettings(loadKeatingUiSettings())` sites (:161, :191, :471) with one `useEffect` using `subscribeKeatingUiSettings`.
- Add `web/src/hooks/use-ui-settings.ts` — `useKeatingUiSettings(): [settings, patch]` wrapping load/subscribe/save; adopt in ProvidersModelsTab and KeatingUiSettingsTab.
- Tests: extend `web/src/test/ui-settings.test.ts` with subscribe-propagation; add hook test.

---

## Part 2 — Unified SessionBrowser

**Breakpoint: 1024px** (single exported const `SESSION_BROWSER_BREAKPOINT`). The 768px side-panel mode dies with the dialog; the sidebar needs 288–640px, which starves chat below 1024.

### Phase 2.1 — `useSessions()` shared data hook
**New** `web/src/hooks/use-sessions.ts`:
```ts
useSessions(opts?: { withHeroes?: boolean; flatLimit?: number }): {
  items, loading, error,
  query, setQuery,
  flatResults,          // non-null while searching (flat, recency-sorted)
  roots,                // buildSessionTree output when not searching
  heroes,               // buildArtifactHeroMap, only when withHeroes
  reload,
  rename(id, title, fromAi?),  // updateSessionTitle + dispatch keating:sessions-changed
  remove(id),                  // sessions.deleteSession + dispatch
}
```
Encapsulates `sessions.getAllMetadata()`, the `keating:sessions-changed` listener, the unified search predicate, and tree building. Talks only to the `sessions`/`keatingStorage` facades → P2P-backend-safe. Flattening/collapse state stays in the desktop consumer.
Test: `web/src/test/use-sessions.test.ts` (mock `sessions` module like `session-fork.test.ts` does).

### Phase 2.2 — SessionBrowser component
**New** `web/src/components/SessionBrowser.tsx` (+ siblings `SessionBrowserDesktop.tsx`, `SessionBrowserSheet.tsx`):
```ts
interface SessionBrowserProps {
  activeSessionId?; forkingSessionId?; forkedSessionId?;
  collapsed?; onCollapsedChange?;          // desktop
  mobileOpen?; onMobileClose?;             // <1024 sheet
  onLoad; onFork; onNewSession?;
  onSuggestTitle?: (id) => Promise<string>; // AI title, both variants
}
```
- **Desktop panel**: lift `SessionSidebar` code (collapsed strip, resize with `keating_session_sidebar_width`, tree with `keating:session-tree-collapsed`, fork buttons) minus data fetching (from hook) and minus "Manage". Add row CRUD parity: Fork stays first-class; rename / delete-confirm / AI-suggest behind a `MoreHorizontal` popover. Port the busy/error/pendingDelete/aiSuggestion state machine from `SessionManagerDialog.tsx:101-181` — moved, not rewritten.
- **Mobile sheet**: lift `SessionCardGrid` wholesale (already a full-screen `fixed inset-0` sheet with masonry, SessionCard + ForkMapCard, rename/delete). Remove "Manage" button; route rename/delete through the hook; keep the non-`role="dialog"` workaround; add `pb-[env(safe-area-inset-bottom)]`.
- **Modify** `SessionCard.tsx`: optional `onSuggestTitle?` prop → Sparkles button that prefills the existing rename input.
- Rename gate: unify on the card's free-rename behavior (`updateSessionTitle(id, title, fromAi || session.aiGeneratedTitle)`); the dialog's aiGeneratedTitle gate was a quirk.

### Phase 2.3 — Wire in, delete old trio
- `useKeatingAgent.tsx`: replace `sessionSidebarElement` (:794-812) with `<SessionBrowser ... onSuggestTitle={suggestSessionTitle} />`; delete `sessionManagerDialogElement` (:773-792), its import, and `sessionsDialog`; keep `openSessions` in the public API but repoint it (mobile → open sheet via store; desktop → uncollapse).
- `Chat.tsx`: `{sessionSidebar}` render point (:482) unchanged (rename var to `sessionBrowser`).
- `keating-agent-store.ts`: no changes — `mobileSidebarOpen` + `keating:session-sidebar-collapsed` reused.
- **Delete**: `SessionManagerDialog.tsx`, `SessionSidebar.tsx`, `SessionCardGrid.tsx`; surgically remove dead `session-manager-*` CSS from `app.css` (don't reformat — it has p2p WIP).
- No storage migration needed; all persisted keys survive unchanged.

---

## Commits (each leaves `bun run build` + `bunx vitest` green)
1. SettingsDialog chips (1.1)
2. Grouped tabs (1.2)
3. Subscribe fix + `useKeatingUiSettings` + tests (1.3)
4. `useSessions` hook + test (2.1)
5. SessionBrowser + wiring + deletions (2.2 + 2.3 together, so the tree never ships two parallel session UIs)

## Risks
- Uncommitted p2p WIP overlaps `useKeatingAgent.tsx` / `Chat.tsx` / `app.css` — stage per-hunk, land settings first.
- Desktop row CRUD clutter → popover cluster; keep ≥40px tap targets.
- Keep `role="dialog"` button-stretch workaround on the mobile sheet.

## Verification
- **Settings**: 3 tabs on desktop sidebar + mobile chips at <640px; section-nav jump links work in all tabs; provider key add/remove, custom model add, proxy save, persona save, speech test, and App toggles all persist across reload; App-tab edits reflect live in Models tab (subscribe fix).
- **Sessions desktop (≥1024)**: resize/collapse/tree-collapse persist; search flattens; load/fork/new/rename/delete/AI-suggest inline; fork animation classes intact.
- **Sessions mobile (<1024)**: hamburger opens full-screen sheet; masonry + ForkMapCard; card rename/delete/fork/suggest; closes on load; safe-area respected; no stretched buttons.
- **Cross-cutting**: fork-from-active captures latest messages; SharedSession share-link flow unchanged; works with P2P backend; `bunx vitest` green; check 375/768/1024/1440px widths via the browser tools.

## Addendum: stretch goals
1. Merge `googleGrounding` + `webSearch` into one setting with a one-time migration in `normalizeUiSettings` (overlapping auto/off semantics today).
2. Decompose the 973-line `ProvidersModelsTab.tsx` into per-section files under `components/settings/`.
3. Full persistence unification — `useKeatingSetting(key)` facade over localStorage + SettingsStore, enabling P2P sync of persona/speech/ui settings.
4. Move `customModels`/`hiddenProviders`/`recentModels` out of the Interface-owned `keating_ui_settings` blob into a provider-scoped store.
5. Shared `useMediaQuery` hook (three ad-hoc `window.matchMedia` copies exist today).
6. Settings deep links (`?settings=proxy`) via the new `defaultTabId` prop.
7. Keyboard navigation for the session tree (`role="tree"` exists, no arrow-key handling); ForkMapCard AI-suggest support.

# Simplify Settings + Unified SessionBrowser

## Context

Two UX problems in the keating web app:

1. **Settings** are spread across 5 tabs (Providers & Models, Teacher Persona, Speech & Voice, Interface, Proxy) with fragmented persistence (3 direct localStorage keys + IndexedDB SettingsStore), cross-tab data ownership (`keating_ui_settings` blob backs the Providers UI), and a stale-state bug (ProvidersModelsTab manually reloads instead of subscribing).
2. **Sessions** have three independently-stateful list UIs off one data source: `SessionSidebar.tsx` (desktop tree, ≥1024px, embeds the grid below that), `SessionCardGrid.tsx` (mobile masonry sheet), and `SessionManagerDialog.tsx` (modal <768px / side panel ≥768px — overlapping the sidebar's breakpoint). Each duplicates items state, search, and `keating:sessions-changed` reload listeners.

User-approved direction: **3 grouped settings tabs** with mobile chip navigation, and **one adaptive SessionBrowser** (desktop sidebar / mobile full-screen sheet) replacing all three session UIs, with the Manage dialog deleted and its CRUD folded in.

Useful validated facts:
- `SessionCard.tsx` already has inline rename + delete-with-confirm; the dialog's only unique capability is AI title suggestion.
- `openSessions`/`sessionsDialog` have no consumers outside `useKeatingAgent.tsx`.
- Working tree has unrelated p2p/electron WIP in `Chat.tsx`, `useKeatingAgent.tsx`, `app.css`, `keating-storage.ts` — stage surgically per phase; never touch `desktop/` or `packages/p2p-core`.

---

## Part 1 — Settings: 5 tabs → 3

Grouping (composition only — no decomposition of existing tab components):

| Tab | Contents |
|---|---|
| **Models & Providers** | ProvidersModelsTab (keys, web search, visibility, custom models, custom providers) + **Proxy** section appended (proxy routes provider requests, so it belongs with keys) |
| **Learning** | Teacher Persona + Speech & Voice (both configure the tutoring experience) |
| **App** | KeatingUiSettingsTab as-is (chat interface, share links, animation renderer, reasoning level, image gen) |

### Phase 1.1 — Mobile chips in SettingsDialog
- `web/src/components/SettingsDialog.tsx`: replace the mobile `<select>` (lines ~76-85) with a horizontal chip row (`sm:hidden flex gap-1.5 overflow-x-auto`, pill buttons with active styling matching the sidebar, `min-h-9`). Chips need `dialog-compact-button` to escape the app.css:402-416 full-width dialog-button rule.
- Add optional `defaultTabId?: string` prop.

### Phase 1.2 — Composed tabs
- **New** `web/src/components/settings/ModelsProvidersTab.tsx` (~30 lines): `<ProvidersModelsTab extraNavSections={[{id:"proxy",label:"Proxy"}]} />` followed by a `#settings-section-proxy` section wrapping `<ProxyTab />` (unchanged).
- **New** `web/src/components/settings/LearningTab.tsx` (~40 lines): merged `SettingsSectionNav` (persona + speech sections), a persona section wrapping `<TeacherPersonaTab />`, then `<SpeechSettingsTab hideNav onSettingsChange={...} />`.
- **Modify** `SpeechSettingsTab.tsx`: add `hideNav?: boolean`, export its sections const.
- **Modify** `ProvidersModelsTab.tsx`: add `extraNavSections?` (spread into existing nav array, ~3 lines).
- **Modify** `KeatingUiSettingsTab.tsx`: add a `SettingsSectionNav` + `settings-section-*` ids for parity (purely additive).
- **Modify** `web/src/hooks/useKeatingAgent.tsx:816-828`: register the 3 tabs (models / learning / app).
- Old tab files stay as building blocks; only registration moves.

### Phase 1.3 — Persistence fix (unification deferred)
Do **not** merge storage mechanisms now (migration risk, no UI payoff). Only:
- Fix the stale-state bug: in `ProvidersModelsTab.tsx`, replace the three manual `setSettings(loadKeatingUiSettings())` sites (:161, :191, :471) with one `useEffect` using `subscribeKeatingUiSettings`.
- Add `web/src/hooks/use-ui-settings.ts` — `useKeatingUiSettings(): [settings, patch]` wrapping load/subscribe/save; adopt in ProvidersModelsTab and KeatingUiSettingsTab.
- Tests: extend `web/src/test/ui-settings.test.ts` with subscribe-propagation; add hook test.

---

## Part 2 — Unified SessionBrowser

**Breakpoint: 1024px** (single exported const `SESSION_BROWSER_BREAKPOINT`). The 768px side-panel mode dies with the dialog; the sidebar needs 288–640px, which starves chat below 1024.

### Phase 2.1 — `useSessions()` shared data hook
**New** `web/src/hooks/use-sessions.ts`:
```ts
useSessions(opts?: { withHeroes?: boolean; flatLimit?: number }): {
  items, loading, error,
  query, setQuery,
  flatResults,          // non-null while searching (flat, recency-sorted)
  roots,                // buildSessionTree output when not searching
  heroes,               // buildArtifactHeroMap, only when withHeroes
  reload,
  rename(id, title, fromAi?),  // updateSessionTitle + dispatch keating:sessions-changed
  remove(id),                  // sessions.deleteSession + dispatch
}
```
Encapsulates `sessions.getAllMetadata()`, the `keating:sessions-changed` listener, the unified search predicate, and tree building. Talks only to the `sessions`/`keatingStorage` facades → P2P-backend-safe. Flattening/collapse state stays in the desktop consumer.
Test: `web/src/test/use-sessions.test.ts` (mock `sessions` module like `session-fork.test.ts` does).

### Phase 2.2 — SessionBrowser component
**New** `web/src/components/SessionBrowser.tsx` (+ siblings `SessionBrowserDesktop.tsx`, `SessionBrowserSheet.tsx`):
```ts
interface SessionBrowserProps {
  activeSessionId?; forkingSessionId?; forkedSessionId?;
  collapsed?; onCollapsedChange?;          // desktop
  mobileOpen?; onMobileClose?;             // <1024 sheet
  onLoad; onFork; onNewSession?;

Add row CRUD parity: Fork stays first-class; rename / delete-confirm / AI-suggest behind a `MoreHorizontal` popover. Port the busy/error/pendingDelete/aiSuggestion state machine from `SessionManagerDialog.tsx:101-181` — moved, not rewritten.
- **Mobile sheet**: lift `SessionCardGrid` wholesale (already a full-screen `fixed inset-0` sheet with masonry, SessionCard + ForkMapCard, rename/delete). Remove "Manage" button; route rename/delete through the hook; keep the non-`role="dialog"` workaround; add `pb-[env(safe-area-inset-bottom)]`.
- **Modify** `SessionCard.tsx`: optional `onSuggestTitle?` prop → Sparkles button that prefills the existing rename input.
- Rename gate: unify on the card's free-rename behavior (`updateSessionTitle(id, title, fromAi || session.aiGeneratedTitle)`); the dialog's aiGeneratedTitle gate was a quirk.

### Phase 2.3 — Wire in, delete old trio
- `useKeatingAgent.tsx`: replace `sessionSidebarElement` (:794-812) with `<SessionBrowser ... onSuggestTitle={suggestSessionTitle} />`; delete `sessionManagerDialogElement` (:773-792), its import, and `sessionsDialog`; keep `openSessions` in the public API but repoint it (mobile → open sheet via store; desktop → uncollapse).
- `Chat.tsx`: `{sessionSidebar}` render point (:482) unchanged (rename var to `sessionBrowser`).
- `keating-agent-store.ts`: no changes — `mobileSidebarOpen` + `keating:session-sidebar-collapsed` reused.
- **Delete**: `SessionManagerDialog.tsx`, `SessionSidebar.tsx`, `SessionCardGrid.tsx`; surgically remove dead `session-manager-*` CSS from `app.css` (don't reformat — it has p2p WIP).
- No storage migration needed; all persisted keys survive unchanged.

---

## Commits (each leaves `bun run build` + `bunx vitest` green)
1. SettingsDialog chips (1.1)
2. Grouped tabs (1.2)
3. Subscribe fix + `useKeatingUiSettings` + tests (1.3)
4. `useSessions` hook + test (2.1)
5. SessionBrowser + wiring + deletions (2.2 + 2.3 together, so the tree never ships two parallel session UIs)

## Risks
- Uncommitted p2p WIP overlaps `useKeatingAgent.tsx` / `Chat.tsx` / `app.css` — stage per-hunk, land settings first.
- Desktop row CRUD clutter → popover cluster; keep ≥40px tap targets.
- Keep `role="dialog"` button-stretch workaround on the mobile sheet.

## Verification
- **Settings**: 3 tabs on desktop sidebar + mobile chips at <640px; section-nav jump links work in all tabs; provider key add/remove, custom model add, proxy save, persona save, speech test, and App toggles all persist across reload; App-tab edits reflect live in Models tab (subscribe fix).
- **Sessions desktop (≥1024)**: resize/collapse/tree-collapse persist; search flattens; load/fork/new/rename/delete/AI-suggest inline; fork animation classes intact.
- **Sessions mobile (<1024)**: hamburger opens full-screen sheet; masonry + ForkMapCard; card rename/delete/fork/suggest; closes on load; safe-area respected; no stretched buttons.
- **Cross-cutting**: fork-from-active captures latest messages; SharedSession share-link flow unchanged; works with P2P backend; `bunx vitest` green; check 375/768/1024/1440px widths via the browser tools.

## Addendum: stretch goals

- **New** `web/src/components/settings/LearningTab.tsx` (~40 lines): merged `SettingsSectionNav` (persona + speech sections), a persona section wrapping `<TeacherPersonaTab />`, then `<SpeechSettingsTab hideNav onSettingsChange={...} />`.

## Part 2 — Unified SessionBrowser

**Breakpoint: 1024px** (single exported const `SESSION_BROWSER_BREAKPOINT`). The 768px side-panel mode dies with the dialog; the sidebar needs 288–640px, which starves chat below 1024.

### Phase 2.1 — `useSessions()` shared data hook
**New** `web/src/hooks/use-sessions.ts`:
```ts
useSessions(opts?: { withHeroes?: boolean; flatLimit?: number }): {
  items, loading, error,
  query, setQuery,
  flatResults,          // non-null while searching (flat, recency-sorted)
  roots,                // buildSessionTree output when not searching
  heroes,               // buildArtifactHeroMap, only when withHeroes
  reload,
  rename(id, title, fromAi?),  // updateSessionTitle + dispatch keating:sessions-changed
  remove(id),                  // sessions.deleteSession + dispatch
}
```
Encapsulates `sessions.getAllMetadata()`, the `keating:sessions-changed` listener, the unified search predicate, and tree building. Talks only to the `sessions`/`keatingStorage` facades → P2P-backend-safe. Flattening/collapse state stays in the desktop consumer.

### Phase 2.2 — SessionBrowser component
**New** `web/src/components/SessionBrowser.tsx` (+ siblings `SessionBrowserDesktop.tsx`, `SessionBrowserSheet.tsx`):
```ts
interface SessionBrowserProps {
  activeSessionId?; forkingSessionId?; forkedSessionId?;
  collapsed?; onCollapsedChange?;          // desktop
  mobileOpen?; onMobileClose?;             // <1024 sheet
  onLoad; onFork; onNewSession?;
  onSuggestTitle?: (id) => Promise<string>; // AI title, both variants
}
```
- **Desktop panel**: lift `SessionSidebar` code (collapsed strip, resize with `keating_session_sidebar_width`, tree with `keating:session-tree-collapsed`, fork buttons) minus data fetching (from hook) and minus "Manage". Add row CRUD parity: Fork stays first-class; rename / delete-confirm / AI-suggest behind a `MoreHorizontal` popover. Port the busy/error/pendingDelete/aiSuggestion state machine from `SessionManagerDialog.tsx:101-181` — moved, not rewritten.
- **Mobile sheet**: lift `SessionCardGrid` wholesale (already a full-screen `fixed inset-0` sheet with masonry, SessionCard + ForkMapCard, rename/delete). Remove "Manage" button; route rename/delete through the hook; keep the non-`role="dialog"` workaround; add `pb-[env(safe-area-inset-bottom)]`.
- **Modify** `SessionCard.tsx`: optional `onSuggestTitle?` prop → Sparkles button that prefills the existing rename input.
- Rename gate: unify on the card's free-rename behavior (`updateSessionTitle(id, title, fromAi || session.aiGeneratedTitle)`); the dialog's aiGeneratedTitle gate was a quirk.

### Phase 2.3 — Wire in, delete old trio
- `useKeatingAgent.tsx`: replace `sessionSidebarElement` (:794-812) with `<SessionBrowser ... onSuggestTitle={suggestSessionTitle} />`; delete `sessionManagerDialogElement` (:773-792), its import, and `sessionsDialog`; keep `openSessions` in the public API but repoint it (mobile → open sheet via store; desktop → uncollapse).
- `Chat.tsx`: `{sessionSidebar}` render point (:482) unchanged (rename var to `sessionBrowser`).
- `keating-agent-store.ts`: no changes — `mobileSidebarOpen` + `keating:session-sidebar-collapsed` reused.
- **Delete**: `SessionManagerDialog.tsx`, `SessionSidebar.tsx`, `SessionCardGrid.tsx`; surgically remove dead `session-manager-*` CSS from `app.css` (don't reformat — it has p2p WIP).
- No storage migration needed; all persisted keys survive unchanged.

---

## Commits (each leaves `bun run build` + `bunx vitest` green)
1. SettingsDialog chips (1.1)
2. Grouped tabs (1.2)
3. Subscribe fix + `useKeatingUiSettings` + tests (1.3)
4. `useSessions` hook + test (2.1)
5. SessionBrowser + wiring + deletions (2.2 + 2.3 together, so the tree never ships two parallel session UIs)

## Risks
- Uncommitted p2p WIP overlaps `useKeatingAgent.tsx` / `Chat.tsx` / `app.css` — stage per-hunk, land settings first.
- Desktop row CRUD clutter → popover cluster; keep ≥40px tap targets.
- Keep `role="dialog"` button-stretch workaround on the mobile sheet.

## Verification
- **Settings**: 3 tabs on desktop sidebar + mobile chips at <640px; section-nav jump links work in all tabs; provider key add/remove, custom model add, proxy save, persona save, speech test, and App toggles all persist across reload; App-tab edits reflect live in Models tab (subscribe fix).
- **Sessions desktop (≥1024)**: resize/collapse/tree-collapse persist; search flattens; load/fork/new/rename/delete/AI-suggest inline; fork animation classes intact.
- **Sessions mobile (<1024)**: hamburger opens full-screen sheet; masonry + ForkMapCard; card rename/delete/fork/suggest; closes on load; safe-area respected; no stretched buttons.
- **Cross-cutting**: fork-from-active captures latest messages; SharedSession share-link flow unchanged; works with P2P backend; `bunx vitest` green; check 375/768/1024/1440px widths via the browser tools.

## Addendum: stretch goals
1. Merge `googleGrounding` + `webSearch` into one setting with a one-time migration in `normalizeUiSettings` (overlapping auto/off semantics today).
2. Decompose the 973-line `ProvidersModelsTab.tsx` into per-section files under `components/settings/`.
3. Full persistence unification — `useKeatingSetting(key)` facade over localStorage + SettingsStore, enabling P2P sync of persona/speech/ui settings.
4. Move `customModels`/`hiddenProviders`/`recentModels` out of the Interface-owned `keating_ui_settings` blob into a provider-scoped store.
5. Shared `useMediaQuery` hook (three ad-hoc `window.matchMedia` copies exist today).
6. Settings deep links (`?settings=proxy`) via the new `defaultTabId` prop.
7. Keyboard navigation for the session tree (`role="tree"` exists, no arrow-key handling); ForkMapCard AI-suggest support.

toolUseResult.plan:
# Simplify Settings + Unified SessionBrowser

## Context

Two UX problems in the keating web app:

1. **Settings** are spread across 5 tabs (Providers & Models, Teacher Persona, Speech & Voice, Interface, Proxy) with fragmented persistence (3 direct localStorage keys + IndexedDB SettingsStore), cross-tab data ownership (`keating_ui_settings` blob backs the Providers UI), and a stale-state bug (ProvidersModelsTab manually reloads instead of subscribing).
2. **Sessions** have three independently-stateful list UIs off one data source: `SessionSidebar.tsx` (desktop tree, ≥1024px, embeds the grid below that), `SessionCardGrid.tsx` (mobile masonry sheet), and `SessionManagerDialog.tsx` (modal <768px / side panel ≥768px — overlapping the sidebar's breakpoint). Each duplicates items state, search, and `keating:sessions-changed` reload listeners.

User-approved direction: **3 grouped settings tabs** with mobile chip navigation, and **one adaptive SessionBrowser** (desktop sidebar / mobile full-screen sheet) replacing all three session UIs, with the Manage dialog deleted and its CRUD folded in.

Useful validated facts:
- `SessionCard.tsx` already has inline rename + delete-with-confirm; the dialog's only unique capability is AI title suggestion.
- `openSessions`/`sessionsDialog` have no consumers outside `useKeatingAgent.tsx`.
- Working tree has unrelated p2p/electron WIP in `Chat.tsx`, `useKeatingAgent.tsx`, `app.css`, `keating-storage.ts` — stage surgically per phase; never touch `desktop/` or `packages/p2p-core`.

---

## Part 1 — Settings: 5 tabs → 3

Grouping (composition only — no decomposition of existing tab components):

| Tab | Contents |
|---|---|
| **Models & Providers** | ProvidersModelsTab (keys, web search, visibility, custom models, custom providers) + **Proxy** section appended (proxy routes provider requests, so it belongs with keys) |
| **Learning** | Teacher Persona + Speech & Voice (both configure the tutoring experience) |
| **App** | KeatingUiSettingsTab as-is (chat interface, share links, animation renderer, reasoning level, image gen) |

### Phase 1.1 — Mobile chips in SettingsDialog
- `web/src/components/SettingsDialog.tsx`: replace the mobile `<select>` (lines ~76-85) with a horizontal chip row (`sm:hidden flex gap-1.5 overflow-x-auto`, pill buttons with active styling matching the sidebar, `min-h-9`). Chips need `dialog-compact-button` to escape the app.css:402-416 full-width dialog-button rule.
- Add optional `defaultTabId?: string` prop.

### Phase 1.2 — Composed tabs
- **New** `web/src/components/settings/ModelsProvidersTab.tsx` (~30 lines): `<ProvidersModelsTab extraNavSections={[{id:"proxy",label:"Proxy"}]} />` followed by a `#settings-section-proxy` section wrapping `<ProxyTab />` (unchanged).
- **New** `web/src/components/settings/LearningTab.tsx` (~40 lines): merged `SettingsSectionNav` (persona + speech sections), a persona section wrapping `<TeacherPersonaTab />`, then `<SpeechSettingsTab hideNav onSettingsChange={...} />`.
- **Modify** `SpeechSettingsTab.tsx`: add `hideNav?: boolean`, export its sections const.
- **Modify** `ProvidersModelsTab.tsx`: add `extraNavSections?` (spread into existing nav array, ~3 lines).
- **Modify** `KeatingUiSettingsTab.tsx`: add a `SettingsSectionNav` + `settings-section-*` ids for parity (purely additive).
- **Modify** `web/src/hooks/useKeatingAgent.tsx:816-828`: register the 3 tabs (models / learning / app).
- Old tab files stay as building blocks; only registration moves.

### Phase 1.3 — Persistence fix (unification deferred)
Do **not** merge storage mechanisms now (migration risk, no UI payoff). Only:
- Fix the stale-state bug: in `ProvidersModelsTab.tsx`, replace the three manual `setSettings(loadKeatingUiSettings())` sites (:161, :191, :471) with one `useEffect` using `subscribeKeatingUiSettings`.
- Add `web/src/hooks/use-ui-settings.ts` — `useKeatingUiSettings(): [settings, patch]` wrapping load/subscribe/save; adopt in ProvidersModelsTab and KeatingUiSettingsTab.
- Tests: extend `web/src/test/ui-settings.test.ts` with subscribe-propagation; add hook test.

---

## Part 2 — Unified SessionBrowser

**Breakpoint: 1024px** (single exported const `SESSION_BROWSER_BREAKPOINT`). The 768px side-panel mode dies with the dialog; the sidebar needs 288–640px, which starves chat below 1024.

### Phase 2.1 — `useSessions()` shared data hook
**New** `web/src/hooks/use-sessions.ts`:
```ts
useSessions(opts?: { withHeroes?: boolean; flatLimit?: number }): {
  items, loading, error,

  flatResults,          // non-null while searching (flat, recency-sorted)

  reload,
  rename(id, title, fromAi?),  // updateSessionTitle + dispatch keating:sessions-changed

Encapsulates `sessions.getAllMetadata()`, the `keating:sessions-changed` listener, the unified search predicate, and tree building. Talks only to the `sessions`/`keatingStorage` facades → P2P-backend-safe. Flattening/collapse state stays in the desktop consumer.
Test: `web/src/test/use-sessions.test.ts` (mock `sessions` module like `session-fork.test.ts` does).

### Phase 2.2 — SessionBrowser component
**New** `web/src/components/SessionBrowser.tsx` (+ siblings `SessionBrowserDesktop.tsx`, `SessionBrowserSheet.tsx`):
```ts
interface SessionBrowserProps {
  activeSessionId?; forkingSessionId?; forkedSessionId?;

Add row CRUD parity: Fork stays first-class; rename / delete-confirm / AI-suggest behind a `MoreHorizontal` popover. Port the busy/error/pendingDelete/aiSuggestion state machine from `SessionManagerDialog.tsx:101-181` — moved, not rewritten.
- **Mobile sheet**: lift `SessionCardGrid` wholesale (already a full-screen `fixed inset-0` sheet with masonry, SessionCard + ForkMapCard, rename/delete). Remove "Manage" button; route rename/delete through the hook; keep the non-`role="dialog"` workaround; add `pb-[env(safe-area-inset-bottom)]`.
- **Modify** `SessionCard.tsx`: optional `onSuggestTitle?` prop → Sparkles button that prefills the existing rename input.
- Rename gate: unify on the card's free-rename behavior (`updateSessionTitle(id, title, fromAi || session.aiGeneratedTitle)`); the dialog's aiGeneratedTitle gate was a quirk.

### Phase 2.3 — Wire in, delete old trio
- `useKeatingAgent.tsx`: replace `sessionSidebarElement` (:794-812) with `<SessionBrowser ... onSuggestTitle={suggestSessionTitle} />`; delete `sessionManagerDialogElement` (:773-792), its import, and `sessionsDialog`; keep `openSessions` in the public API but repoint it (mobile → open sheet via store; desktop → uncollapse).
- `Chat.tsx`: `{sessionSidebar}` render point (:482) unchanged (rename var to `sessionBrowser`).
- `keating-agent-store.ts`: no changes — `mobileSidebarOpen` + `keating:session-sidebar-collapsed` reused.
- **Delete**: `SessionManagerDialog.tsx`, `SessionSidebar.tsx`, `SessionCardGrid.tsx`; surgically remove dead `session-manager-*` CSS from `app.css` (don't reformat — it has p2p WIP).
- No storage migration needed; all persisted keys survive unchanged.

---

## Commits (each leaves `bun run build` + `bunx vitest` green)
1. SettingsDialog chips (1.1)
2. Grouped tabs (1.2)
3. Subscribe fix + `useKeatingUiSettings` + tests (1.3)
4. `useSessions` hook + test (2.1)
5. SessionBrowser + wiring + deletions (2.2 + 2.3 together, so the tree never ships two parallel session UIs)

## Addendum: stretch goals

- Tests: extend `web/src/test/ui-settings.test.ts` with: saving settings notifies a subscriber registered via `subscribeKeatingUiSettings`. Add `web/src/test/use-ui-settings.test.ts` exercising the hook (renderHook from @testing-library/react if available in web/package.json — check first; if not available, test the underlying subscribe/save contract instead, do NOT add new dependencies).

## Acceptance

    # Run a simple prompt                                                  
    crush run "Guess my 5 favorite Pokémon"                                
                                                                           
    # Pipe input from stdin

    # Continue the most recent session

    -D --data-dir  Custom crush data directory

# Phase 2 spec — Unified SessionBrowser (keating web app)

Repo: /home/diogenes/Projects/keating. React 19 + Tailwind v4 + Vite; tests: `cd web && bunx vitest run`; typecheck: `cd web && bunx tsc --noEmit` (or repo script).
Do NOT touch `desktop/`, `packages/p2p-core/`. `web/src/pages/Chat.tsx` and `web/src/app.css` have unrelated WIP — make only the minimal edits listed for them, nothing else in those files.

Goal: replace THREE session UIs (`SessionSidebar.tsx` desktop tree ≥1024px, `SessionCardGrid.tsx` mobile masonry sheet, `SessionManagerDialog.tsx` modal/side-panel CRUD dialog) with ONE adaptive `SessionBrowser` + a shared data hook. Pre-designed skeleton files exist (see below) — fill them in, keep their exported APIs exactly.

## Step 1 — implement `web/src/hooks/use-sessions.ts` (skeleton exists)
Implement the `useSessions` hook per the interface in the skeleton:
- Data: `sessions.getAllMetadata()` from `../hooks/keating-storage` (import path per skeleton), sorted recency desc like existing components.
- Heroes: when `opts.withHeroes`, also `buildArtifactHeroMap(keatingStorage)` exactly as `SessionCardGrid.tsx` lines ~70-80 does (catch → empty Map).
- Reload on `keating:sessions-changed` window event; cleanup on unmount.
- Search: port the filter predicate(s) used by SessionSidebar/SessionCardGrid/SessionManagerDialog (union: title, preview, relative date, message count where present). While `query` non-empty → `flatResults` (flat, recency-sorted, capped at `opts.flatLimit ?? 50`); else `flatResults = null` and `roots` = `buildSessionTree(items)` from `../components/session-tree`.
- `rename(id, title, fromAi?)` → use `updateSessionTitle` from `./keating-storage` (it already re-derives metadata and dispatches the changed event — verify signature).
- `remove(id)` → `sessions.delete(...)`/`deleteSession` (check the exact SessionsStore API used by SessionManagerDialog) then dispatch `keating:sessions-changed`.
- Test: `web/src/test/use-sessions.test.ts` — mock the storage module like `web/src/test/session-fork.test.ts` does; cover: initial load, event-driven reload, search flat/tree switching, remove dispatches event. No new dependencies.

## Step 2 — implement SessionBrowser (skeletons exist)
Files (pre-designed, keep exported names/props):
- `web/src/components/SessionBrowser.tsx` — top-level adaptive component; owns `useSessions({ withHeroes: !isDesktop })` and a `window.matchMedia("(min-width: 1024px)")` listener (`SESSION_BROWSER_BREAKPOINT` exported const). Renders `<SessionBrowserDesktop/>` at ≥1024px else `<SessionBrowserSheet/>`.
- `web/src/components/SessionBrowserDesktop.tsx` — port the desktop panel from `SessionSidebar.tsx` essentially verbatim: collapsed 56px strip mode, drag-resize 288–640px persisted at localStorage `keating_session_sidebar_width`, `role="tree"` flattened fork tree with collapse state persisted at `keating:session-tree-collapsed`, search box, New Session button, active-session highlight, fork button + fork-arrival animation classes (`session-fork-arrive`, "Forked" chip). REMOVE: the "Manage" button and the embedded SessionCardGrid delegation (mobile is handled by the sheet now). ADD per-row CRUD parity with the old SessionManagerDialog: Fork stays as the existing inline button; add a `MoreHorizontal` (lucide) popover per row containing: Rename (inline input), Suggest title (Sparkles, calls `onSuggestTitle`, prefills the rename input with the result), Delete (two-step inline confirm). Port the busy/error/pendingDelete/aiSuggestion state handling from `SessionManagerDialog.tsx` lines ~101-181 — move the logic, don't reinvent. Rename is ALWAYS enabled (drop the old aiGeneratedTitle gate; when saving pass `fromAi=false` semantics like SessionCard does — see updateSessionTitle usage).
- `web/src/components/SessionBrowserSheet.tsx` — port `SessionCardGrid.tsx` essentially verbatim (full-screen `fixed inset-0` sheet, masonry, `SessionCard` + `ForkMapCard`, search, New Session): REMOVE the "Manage" button; data comes from the shared hook via props instead of its own state; rename/delete route through the hook's `rename`/`remove`; keep the deliberate absence of `role="dialog"` (app.css full-width-button rule) — preserve the existing comment; ensure bottom scroll padding includes `pb-[env(safe-area-inset-bottom)]`.
- `web/src/components/SessionCard.tsx` — add optional `onSuggestTitle?: (id: string) => Promise<string>`; when present render a Sparkles button in the existing action row that awaits it and enters the existing rename mode with the draft prefilled. Keep everything else.

## Step 3 — wire in and delete
- `web/src/hooks/useKeatingAgent.tsx`:
  - Replace `sessionSidebarElement` (~lines 794-812) with `<SessionBrowser activeSessionId=... forkingSessionId=... forkedSessionId=... collapsed=... onCollapsedChange=... mobileOpen=... onMobileClose=... onLoad={loadSession} onFork={forkSession} onNewSession={newSession} onSuggestTitle={<existing AI title suggestion fn ~line 720>} />` — same wiring the old sidebar got, plus suggest.
  - Delete `sessionManagerDialogElement` (~lines 773-792), its `SessionManagerDialog` import, and the `sessionsDialog` dialog state.
  - Keep `openSessions` in the returned API but repoint: below 1024px open the mobile sheet (store's toggle/open action), at ≥1024px un-collapse the sidebar.
  - Mobile open state and desktop collapsed state continue to come from `web/src/stores/keating-agent-store.ts` (`mobileSidebarOpen`, `sessionSidebarCollapsed`) — no store changes.
- `web/src/pages/Chat.tsx`: ONLY if it references the removed dialog element individually, adjust; the `{sessionSidebar}` render position stays. No other edits (WIP file).
- DELETE files: `web/src/components/SessionManagerDialog.tsx`, `web/src/components/SessionSidebar.tsx`, `web/src/components/SessionCardGrid.tsx`. Grep for remaining imports of these (including tests) and update.
- `web/src/app.css`: remove only CSS rules that reference the deleted dialog (search `session-manager`) — surgical line deletions only, no reformatting (WIP file). If unsure a rule is dead, leave it.

## Acceptance
- Typecheck + `bunx vitest run` green from web/.
- No references to SessionSidebar/SessionCardGrid/SessionManagerDialog remain anywhere in web/src.
- Existing tests `session-fork.test.ts`, `fork-map-layout.test.ts`, `session-card-visuals.test.ts` untouched and passing.
- Print summary of files changed/deleted.

Implement the `useSessions` hook per the interface in the skeleton:
- Data: `sessions.getAllMetadata()` from `../hooks/keating-storage` (import path per skeleton), sorted recency desc like existing components.
- Heroes: when `opts.withHeroes`, also `buildArtifactHeroMap(keatingStorage)` exactly as `SessionCardGrid.tsx` lines ~70-80 does (catch → empty Map).
- Reload on `keating:sessions-changed` window event; cleanup on unmount.
- Search: port the filter predicate(s) used by SessionSidebar/SessionCardGrid/SessionManagerDialog (union: title, preview, relative date, message count where present). While `query` non-empty → `flatResults` (flat, recency-sorted, capped at `opts.flatLimit ?? 50`); else `flatResults = null` and `roots` = `buildSessionTree(items)` from `../components/session-tree`.
- `rename(id, title, fromAi?)` → use `updateSessionTitle` from `./keating-storage` (it already re-derives metadata and dispatches the changed event — verify signature).
- `remove(id)` → `sessions.delete(...)`/`deleteSession` (check the exact SessionsStore API used by SessionManagerDialog) then dispatch `keating:sessions-changed`.
- Test: `web/src/test/use-sessions.test.ts` — mock the storage module like `web/src/test/session-fork.test.ts` does; cover: initial load, event-driven reload, search flat/tree switching, remove dispatches event. No new dependencies.

## Step 2 — implement SessionBrowser (skeletons exist)
Files (pre-designed, keep exported names/props):
- `web/src/components/SessionBrowser.tsx` — top-level adaptive component; owns `useSessions({ withHeroes: !isDesktop })` and a `window.matchMedia("(min-width: 1024px)")` listener (`SESSION_BROWSER_BREAKPOINT` exported const). Renders `<SessionBrowserDesktop/>` at ≥1024px else `<SessionBrowserSheet/>`.
- `web/src/components/SessionBrowserDesktop.tsx` — port the desktop panel from `SessionSidebar.tsx` essentially verbatim: collapsed 56px strip mode, drag-resize 288–640px persisted at localStorage `keating_session_sidebar_width`, `role="tree"` flattened fork tree with collapse state persisted at `keating:session-tree-collapsed`, search box, New Session button, active-session highlight, fork button + fork-arrival animation classes (`session-fork-arrive`, "Forked" chip). REMOVE: the "Manage" button and the embedded SessionCardGrid delegation (mobile is handled by the sheet now). ADD per-row CRUD parity with the old SessionManagerDialog: Fork stays as the existing inline button; add a `MoreHorizontal` (lucide) popover per row containing: Rename (inline input), Suggest title (Sparkles, calls `onSuggestTitle`, prefills the rename input with the result), Delete (two-step inline confirm). Port the busy/error/pendingDelete/aiSuggestion state handling from `SessionManagerDialog.tsx` lines ~101-181 — move the logic, don't reinvent. Rename is ALWAYS enabled (drop the old aiGeneratedTitle gate; when saving pass `fromAi=false` semantics like SessionCard does — see updateSessionTitle usage).
- `web/src/components/SessionBrowserSheet.tsx` — port `SessionCardGrid.tsx` essentially verbatim (full-screen `fixed inset-0` sheet, masonry, `SessionCard` + `ForkMapCard`, search, New Session): REMOVE the "Manage" button; data comes from the shared hook via props instead of its own state; rename/delete route through the hook's `rename`/`remove`; keep the deliberate absence of `role="dialog"` (app.css full-width-button rule) — preserve the existing comment; ensure bottom scroll padding includes `pb-[env(safe-area-inset-bottom)]`.
- `web/src/components/SessionCard.tsx` — add optional `onSuggestTitle?: (id: string) => Promise<string>`; when present render a Sparkles button in the existing action row that awaits it and enters the existing rename mode with the draft prefilled. Keep everything else.

## Step 3 — wire in and delete
- `web/src/hooks/useKeatingAgent.tsx`:
  - Replace `sessionSidebarElement` (~lines 794-812) with `<SessionBrowser activeSessionId=... forkingSessionId=... forkedSessionId=... collapsed=... onCollapsedChange=... mobileOpen=... onMobileClose=... onLoad={loadSession} onFork={forkSession} onNewSession={newSession} onSuggestTitle={<existing AI title suggestion fn ~line 720>} />` — same wiring the old sidebar got, plus suggest.
  - Delete `sessionManagerDialogElement` (~lines 773-792), its `SessionManagerDialog` import, and the `sessionsDialog` dialog state.
  - Keep `openSessions` in the returned API but repoint: below 1024px open the mobile sheet (store's toggle/open action), at ≥1024px un-collapse the sidebar.
  - Mobile open state and desktop collapsed state continue to come from `web/src/stores/keating-agent-store.ts` (`mobileSidebarOpen`, `sessionSidebarCollapsed`) — no store changes.
- `web/src/pages/Chat.tsx`: ONLY if it references the removed dialog element individually, adjust; the `{sessionSidebar}` render position stays. No other edits (WIP file).
- DELETE files: `web/src/components/SessionManagerDialog.tsx`, `web/src/components/SessionSidebar.tsx`, `web/src/components/SessionCardGrid.tsx`. Grep for remaining imports of these (including tests) and update.
- `web/src/app.css`: remove only CSS rules that reference the deleted dialog (search `session-manager`) — surgical line deletions only, no reformatting (WIP file). If unsure a rule is dead, leave it.

## Acceptance
- Typecheck + `bunx vitest run` green from web/.
- No references to SessionSidebar/SessionCardGrid/SessionManagerDialog remain anywhere in web/src.
- Existing tests `session-fork.test.ts`, `fork-map-layout.test.ts`, `session-card-visuals.test.ts` untouched and passing.
- Print summary of files changed/deleted.

# Phase 1b spec — remaining settings work (keating)

Repo: /home/diogenes/Projects/keating. Typecheck: `cd web && bunx tsc --noEmit`. Tests: `cd web && bun test` (bun's test runner, NOT vitest).
Do NOT touch: `desktop/`, `packages/p2p-core/`, `web/src/pages/Chat.tsx`, `web/src/app.css`, `web/src/hooks/keating-storage.ts`.
Already DONE (do not redo): SettingsDialog chips + defaultTabId; SpeechSettingsTab SPEECH_SECTIONS/hideNav; ProvidersModelsTab extraNavSections; KeatingUiSettingsTab section nav; new composed tabs `web/src/components/settings/ModelsProvidersTab.tsx` and `web/src/components/settings/LearningTab.tsx`.

## Task A — register the 3 grouped tabs
File: `web/src/hooks/useKeatingAgent.tsx` — find the `<SettingsDialog ... tabs={[...]}` registration (~lines 816-828). Replace the current 5 tabs with exactly:
- `{ id: "models", label: "Models & Providers", component: <ModelsProvidersTab /> }` (import from `../components/settings/ModelsProvidersTab`)
- `{ id: "learning", label: "Learning", component: <LearningTab onSpeechSettingsChange={...} /> }` (import from `../components/settings/LearningTab`) — pass the same callback the current SpeechSettingsTab entry passes as `onSettingsChange`; preserve that wiring.
- `{ id: "app", label: "App", component: <KeatingUiSettingsTab /> }`
Remove imports that become unused in this file (TeacherPersonaTab, SpeechSettingsTab, ProxyTab, ProvidersModelsTab).

## Task B — useKeatingUiSettings hook + stale-state fix
1. New file `web/src/hooks/use-ui-settings.ts`:
   `export function useKeatingUiSettings(): [KeatingUiSettings, (patch: Partial<KeatingUiSettings>) => void]`
   - state init from `loadKeatingUiSettings()`; effect subscribes with `subscribeKeatingUiSettings` (read `web/src/keating/ui-settings.ts` first for exact signatures/unsubscribe contract); the patch fn merges onto the latest settings and calls `saveKeatingUiSettings`.
2. `web/src/components/ProvidersModelsTab.tsx`: replace its `useState(() => loadKeatingUiSettings())` settings state with the hook, and DELETE the manual `setSettings(loadKeatingUiSettings())` reload calls after mutating helpers (~3 sites). If any helper it calls (toggleProviderVisibility, addCustomModel, removeCustomModel in ui-settings.ts) does not dispatch the change event on save, fix the helper to dispatch rather than keeping manual reloads. Careful: keep local draft state (add-model form etc.) untouched.
3. `web/src/components/KeatingUiSettingsTab.tsx`: if it hand-rolls load/subscribe/save for the same settings, adopt the hook; otherwise leave it.

## Task C — tests (bun test)
- Extend `web/src/test/ui-settings.test.ts`: saving via `saveKeatingUiSettings` notifies a subscriber registered with `subscribeKeatingUiSettings`; unsubscribe stops notifications. Match the existing test file's setup (it already handles localStorage/window shims — reuse its patterns; check how existing tests fake `window`).
- Only if trivially possible with existing dev deps (check web/package.json — do NOT add dependencies): a small test for `useKeatingUiSettings`. If React hook testing isn't feasible with current deps, skip it and say so.

## Acceptance
- `cd web && bunx tsc --noEmit` clean; `cd web && bun test` green.
- Settings dialog registers exactly 3 tabs.
- Print a list of files changed.

- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud (requires claude.ai account access)). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- claude-in-chrome: Automates your Chrome browser to interact with web pages - clicking elements, filling forms, capturing screenshots, reading console logs, and navigating sites. Opens pages in new tabs within your existing Chrome session. Requires site-level permissions before executing (configured in the extension). - When the user wants to interact with web pages, automate browser tasks, capture screenshots, read console logs, or perform any browser-based actions. Always invoke BEFORE attempting to use any mcp__claude-in-chrome__* tools.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

# Phase 1b spec — remaining settings work (keating)

Repo: /home/diogenes/Projects/keating. Typecheck: `cd web && bunx tsc --noEmit`. Tests: `cd web && bun test` (bun's test runner, NOT vitest).
Do NOT touch: `desktop/`, `packages/p2p-core/`, `web/src/pages/Chat.tsx`, `web/src/app.css`, `web/src/hooks/keating-storage.ts`.
Already DONE (do not redo): SettingsDialog chips + defaultTabId; SpeechSettingsTab SPEECH_SECTIONS/hideNav; ProvidersModelsTab extraNavSections; KeatingUiSettingsTab section nav; new composed tabs `web/src/components/settings/ModelsProvidersTab.tsx` and `web/src/components/settings/LearningTab.tsx`.

## Task A — register the 3 grouped tabs
File: `web/src/hooks/useKeatingAgent.tsx` — find the `<SettingsDialog ... tabs={[...]}` registration (~lines 816-828). Replace the current 5 tabs with exactly:
- `{ id: "models", label: "Models & Providers", component: <ModelsProvidersTab /> }` (import from `../components/settings/ModelsProvidersTab`)
- `{ id: "learning", label: "Learning", component: <LearningTab onSpeechSettingsChange={...} /> }` (import from `../components/settings/LearningTab`) — pass the same callback the current SpeechSettingsTab entry passes as `onSettingsChange`; preserve that wiring.
- `{ id: "app", label: "App", component: <KeatingUiSettingsTab /> }`
Remove imports that become unused in this file (TeacherPersonaTab, SpeechSettingsTab, ProxyTab, ProvidersModelsTab).

## Task B — useKeatingUiSettings hook + stale-state fix
1. New file `web/src/hooks/use-ui-settings.ts`:
   `export function useKeatingUiSettings(): [KeatingUiSettings, (patch: Partial<KeatingUiSettings>) => void]`
   - state init from `loadKeatingUiSettings()`; effect subscribes with `subscribeKeatingUiSettings` (read `web/src/keating/ui-settings.ts` first for exact signatures/unsubscribe contract); the patch fn merges onto the latest settings and calls `saveKeatingUiSettings`.
2. `web/src/components/ProvidersModelsTab.tsx`: replace its `useState(() => loadKeatingUiSettings())` settings state with the hook, and DELETE the 3 manual `setSettings(loadKeatingUiSettings())` reload calls after mutating helpers. If any helper it calls (toggleProviderVisibility, addCustomModel, removeCustomModel in ui-settings.ts) does not dispatch the change event on save, fix the helper to dispatch rather than keeping manual reloads. Careful: keep local draft state (add-model form etc.) untouched.
3. `web/src/components/KeatingUiSettingsTab.tsx`: if it hand-rolls load/subscribe/save for the same settings, adopt the hook; otherwise leave it.

## Task C — tests (bun test)
- Extend `web/src/test/ui-settings.test.ts`: saving via `saveKeatingUiSettings` notifies a subscriber registered with `subscribeKeatingUiSettings`; unsubscribe stops notifications. Match the existing test file's setup (it already handles localStorage/window shims — reuse its patterns).
- Only if trivially possible with existing dev deps (check web/package.json — do NOT add dependencies): a small test for `useKeatingUiSettings`. If React hook testing isn't feasible with current deps, skip it and say so.

## Acceptance
- `cd web && bunx tsc --noEmit` clean; `cd web && bun test` green.
- Settings dialog registers exactly 3 tabs.
- Print a list of files changed.

input.content:
# Phase 2 spec — Unified SessionBrowser (keating web app)

Repo: /home/diogenes/Projects/keating. Typecheck: `cd web && bunx tsc --noEmit`. Tests: `cd web && bun test` (bun test runner, NOT vitest). Do NOT add dependencies.
Do NOT touch `desktop/`, `packages/p2p-core/`. `web/src/pages/Chat.tsx` and `web/src/app.css` carry unrelated WIP — only the minimal edits listed below, nothing else.

Goal: replace THREE session UIs (`SessionSidebar.tsx` desktop tree ≥1024px, `SessionCardGrid.tsx` mobile masonry sheet, `SessionManagerDialog.tsx` CRUD dialog/side-panel) with ONE adaptive `SessionBrowser` + one shared data hook.

DESIGNED SKELETONS ALREADY EXIST — keep their exported names, props, and doc comments exactly; replace the `throw`/TODO bodies with implementations:
- `web/src/hooks/use-sessions.ts` (interface final; TODO list in the body is the implementation checklist)
- `web/src/components/SessionBrowser.tsx` (COMPLETE — do not modify except if a compile error forces it)
- `web/src/components/SessionBrowserDesktop.tsx` (port target: SessionSidebar + SessionManagerDialog CRUD)
- `web/src/components/SessionBrowserSheet.tsx` (port target: SessionCardGrid)

## Step 1 — implement useSessions
Follow the TODO checklist inside `use-sessions.ts`. Key facts verified for you:
- `sessions.getAllMetadata()`, `sessions.deleteSession(id)` (see SessionManagerDialog.tsx:133), `updateSessionTitle(id, title, aiGeneratedTitle?)` from `../hooks/keating-storage` (it already dispatches `keating:sessions-changed`).

Terms of Service
